name: Artificer
description: Artificers are powerful magic wielders who are experts at creating and forging magic items and potions to assist their allies or bring doom to their foes.

subclasses.name: Armorer
subclasses.description: Fuse powerful magic with your armor to create exoskeleton suits

features.name: Arcane Armor
features.description: As an action with smith's tools, you turn worn armor into Arcane Armor: it has no Strength requirement for you, serves as your artificer focus, covers your body, can replace missing limbs, and cannot be removed against your will.

features.name: Armor Model
features.description: Each model includes a special weapon. When you attack with that weapon, you may add your Intelligence modifier, instead of Strength or Dexterity, to the attack and damage rolls. You may change the armor's model whenever you finish a short or long rest, provided you have smith's tools in hand.

features.name: Armorer Spells
features.description: Adds subclass spells at the listed levels: 3rd: magic missile, thunderwave; 5th: mirror image, shatter; 9th: hypnotic pattern, lightning bolt; 13th: fire shield, greater invisibility; 17th: passwall, wall of force.

features.name: Dampening Field
features.description: You have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks. If the armor normally imposes disadvantage on such checks, the advantage and disadvantage cancel each other, as normal.

features.name: Defensive Field
features.description: As a bonus action, you may gain temporary hit points equal to your level in this class, replacing any temporary hit points you already have. You lose these temporary hit points if you doff the armor. You may use this bonus action a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Lightning Launcher
features.description: It counts as a simple ranged weapon, with a normal range of 90 feet and a long range of 300 feet, and it deals 1d6 lightning damage on a hit. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with it, you may deal an extra 1d6 lightning damage to that target.

features.name: Powered Steps
features.description: Your walking speed increases by 5 feet.

features.name: Thunder Gauntlets
features.description: Each of the armor's gauntlets counts as a simple melee weapon while you aren't holding anything in it, and it deals 1d8 thunder damage on a hit. A creature hit by the gauntlet has disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you until the start of your next turn, as the armor magically emits a distracting pulse when the creature attacks someone else.

features.name: Tools of the Trade
features.description: You receive proficiency with heavy armor. You also gain proficiency with smith's tools. If you already have this tool proficiency, you receive proficiency with one other type of artisan's tools of your choice.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Armor Modifications
features.description: You learn how to use your artificer infusions to specially modify your Arcane Armor. That armor now counts as separate items for the purposes of your Infuse Items feature: armor (the chest piece), boots, helmet, and the armor's special weapon. Each of those items can bear one of your infusions, and the infusions transfer over if you change your armor's model with the Armor Model feature.

features.name: Guardian
features.description: When a Huge or smaller creature you may see ends its turn within 30 feet of you, you may use your reaction to magically force it to make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, you pull the creature up to 25 feet directly to an unoccupied space. If you pull the target to a space within 5 feet of you, you may make a melee weapon attack against it as part of this reaction.

features.name: Infiltrator
features.description: Any creature that takes lightning damage from your Lightning Launcher glimmers with magical light until the start of your next turn. The glimmering creature sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius, and it has disadvantage on attack rolls against you, as the light jolts it if it attacks you. Also, the next attack roll against it has advantage, and if that attack hits, the target takes an extra 1d6 lightning damage.

features.name: Perfected Armor
features.description: Improves both Arcane Armor models; Guardian can pull a nearby creature and strike it, while Infiltrator marks lightning-damaged targets with light, disadvantage against you, and an opening for extra damage.

subclasses.name: Alchemist
subclasses.description: A master of potion-making to heal, assist, and destroy

features.name: Alchemist Spells
features.description: Adds subclass spells at the listed levels: 3rd: healing word, ray of sickness; 5th: flaming sphere, Melf's acid arrow; 9th: gaseous form, mass healing word; 13th: blight, death ward; 17th: cloudkill, raise dead.

features.name: Experimental Elixir
features.description: Whenever you finish a long rest, you may magically produce an experimental elixir in an empty flask you touch. Roll on the Experimental Elixir table for the elixir's effect, which is triggered when someone drinks the elixir. As an action, a creature can drink the elixir or administer it to an incapacitated creature.

features.name: Tool Proficiency
features.description: When you adopt this specialization at 3rd level, you receive proficiency with alchemist's supplies. If you already have this proficiency, you receive proficiency with one other type of artisan's tools of your choice.

features.name: Alchemical Savant
features.description: You develop masterful command of magical chemicals, enhancing the healing and damage you create through them. Whenever you cast a spell using your alchemist's supplies as the spellcasting focus, you receive a bonus to one roll of the spell. That roll must restore hit points or be a damage roll that deals acid, fire, necrotic, or poison damage, and the bonus equals your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1).

features.name: Restorative Reagents
features.description: You may incorporate restorative reagents into some of your works: Whenever a creature drinks an experimental elixir you created, the creature gains temporary hit points equal to 2d6 + your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1 temporary hit point). You may cast lesser restoration without expending a spell slot and without preparing the spell, provided you use alchemist's supplies as the spellcasting focus.

features.name: Chemical Mastery
features.description: By 15th level, you have been exposed to so many chemicals that they pose little risk to you, and you may use them to quickly end certain ailments: You receive resistance to acid damage and poison damage, and you are immune to the poisoned condition.

subclasses.name: Artillerist
subclasses.description: Control the battlefield by summoning powerful magic cannons

features.name: Artillerist Spells
features.description: Adds subclass spells at the listed levels: 3rd: shield, thunderwave; 5th: scorching ray, shatter; 9th: fireball, wind wall; 13th: ice storm, wall of fire; 17th: cone of cold, wall of force.

features.name: Eldritch Cannon
features.description: You learn how to create a magical cannon. Using woodcarver's tools or smith's tools, you may take an action to magically create a Small or Tiny eldritch cannon in an unoccupied space on a horizontal surface within 5 feet of you. A Small eldritch cannon occupies its space, and a Tiny one can be held in one hand.

features.name: Tool Proficiency
features.description: When you adopt this specialization at 3rd level, you receive proficiency with woodcarver's tools. If you already have this proficiency, you receive proficiency with one other type of artisan's tools of your choice.

features.name: Arcane Firearm
features.description: You know how to turn a wand, staff, or rod into an arcane firearm, a conduit for your destructive spells. When you finish a long rest, you may use woodcarver's tools to carve special sigils into a wand, staff, or rod and thereby turn it into your arcane firearm. You may use your arcane firearm as a spellcasting focus for your artificer spells.

features.name: Explosive Cannon
features.description: Every eldritch cannon you create is more destructive: The cannon's damage rolls all increase by 1d8. As an action, you may command the cannon to detonate if you are within 60 feet of it. Doing so destroys the cannon and forces each creature within 20 feet of it to make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC, taking 3d8 force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

features.name: Fortified Position
features.description: You're a master at forming well-defended emplacements using Eldritch Cannon: You and your allies have Cover while within 10 feet of a cannon you create with Eldritch Cannon, as a result of a shimmering field of magical protection that the cannon emits. You may create two with the same action (but not the same spell slot), and you may activate both of them with the same bonus action.

subclasses.name: Battle Smith
subclasses.description: Creating magical machines that fight and protect others.

features.name: Battle Ready
features.description: When you reach 3rd level, your combat training and your experiments with magic have paid off in two ways: You receive proficiency with martial weapons. When you attack with a magic weapon, you may use your Intelligence modifier, instead of Strength or Dexterity modifier, for the attack and damage rolls.

features.name: Battle Smith Spells
features.description: Adds subclass spells at the listed levels: 3rd: heroism, shield; 5th: branding smite, warding bond; 9th: aura of vitality, conjure barrage; 13th: aura of purity, fire shield; 17th: banishing smite, mass cure wounds.

features.name: Steel Defender
features.description: See this creature's game statistics in the steel defender stat block, which uses your proficiency bonus (PB) in several places. In combat, the defender shares your initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It can move and use its reaction on its own, but the only action it takes on its turn is the Dodge action, unless you take a bonus action on your turn to command it to take another action.

features.name: Tool Proficiency
features.description: When you adopt this specialization at 3rd level, you receive proficiency with smith's tools. If you already have this proficiency, you receive proficiency with one other type of artisan's tools of your choice.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Arcane Jolt
features.description: You learn new ways to channel arcane energy to harm or heal. When either you hit a target with a magic weapon attack or your steel defender hits a target, you may channel magical energy through the strike to create one of the following effects: The target takes an extra 2d6 force damage. Choose one creature or object you may see within 30 feet of the target.

features.name: Improved Defender
features.description: Your Arcane Jolt and steel defender become more powerful: The extra damage and the healing of your Arcane Jolt both increase to 4d6. Your steel defender gains a +2 bonus to Armor Class. Whenever your steel defender uses its Deflect Attack, the attacker takes force damage equal to 1d4 + your Intelligence modifier.

name: Barbarian
description: Barbarians are powerful warriors whose strength comes from their rage. Able to use their anger as a weapon. The subclass represents different paths they walk, which teaches them special ways to use their anger.

subclasses.name: Berserker
subclasses.description: Fall into your rage entirely to deliver a tide of powerful blows.

features.name: Frenzy
features.description: You may go into a frenzy when you rage. If you do, for the duration of your rage you may make a single melee weapon attack as a bonus action on each of your turns after this one. When your rage ends, you suffer one level of exhaustion.

features.name: Mindless Rage
features.description: You can't be charmed or frightened while raging. If you are charmed or frightened when you enter your rage, the effect is suspended for the duration of the rage.

features.name: Intimidating Presence
features.description: You may use your action to frighten someone with your menacing presence. When you do so, choose one creature that you may see within 30 feet of you. If the creature can see or hear you, it must pass a Wisdom saving throw (DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier) or be frightened of you until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Retaliation
features.description: When you take damage from a creature that is within 5 feet of you, you may use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature.

subclasses.name: Totem Warrior
subclasses.description: Your rage comes from the animal spirits of the world who aid.

features.name: Bear
features.description: While raging, you have resistance to all damage except psychic damage.

features.name: Eagle
features.description: While you're raging and aren't wearing heavy armor, other creatures have disadvantage on opportunity attack rolls against you, and you may use the Dash action as a bonus action on your turn.

features.name: Elk
features.description: While you're raging and aren't wearing heavy armor, your walking speed increases by 15 feet. The spirit of the elk makes you extraordinarily swift.

features.name: Spirit Seeker
features.description: Yours is a path that seeks attunement with the natural world, giving you a kinship with beasts. At 3rd level when you adopt this path, you receive the ability to cast the beast sense and speak with animals spells, but only as rituals, as described in chapter 10.

features.name: Tiger
features.description: While raging, you may add 10 feet to your long jump distance and 3 feet to your high jump distance.

features.name: Totem Spirit
features.description: You makes or acquire a physical totem object-an amulet or similar adornment-that incorporates fur or feathers, claws, teeth, or bones of the totem animal. For example, if you have a bear totem spirit, you might be unusually hairy and thick-skinned, or if your totem is the eagle, your eyes turn bright yellow. For example, you could choose a hawk or vulture in place of an eagle.

features.name: Wolf
features.description: While you're raging, your friends have advantage on melee attack rolls against any creature within 5 feet of you that is hostile to you.

features.name: Aspect of the Beast
features.description: Grants the listed option features for this subclass feature at this level.

features.name: Bear
features.description: Your carrying capacity (including maximum load and maximum lift) is doubled, and you have advantage on Strength checks made to push, pull, lift, or break objects.

features.name: Eagle
features.description: You may see up to 1 mile away with no difficulty, able to discern even fine details as though looking at something no more than 100 feet away from you. Also, dim light doesn't impose disadvantage on your Wisdom (Perception) checks.

features.name: Elk
features.description: Whether mounted or on foot, your travel pace is doubled, as is the travel pace of up to ten companions while they're within 60 feet of you and you're not incapacitated.

features.name: Tiger
features.description: You receive proficiency in two skills from the following list: Athletics, Acrobatics, Stealth, and Survival.

features.name: Wolf
features.description: You may track other creatures while traveling at a fast pace, and you may move stealthily while traveling at a normal pace.

features.name: Spirit Walker
features.description: You may cast the commune with nature spell, but only as a ritual.

features.name: Bear
features.description: While you're raging, any creature within 5 feet of you that's hostile to you has disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you or another character with this feature. An enemy is immune to this effect if it can't see or hear you or if it can't be frightened.

features.name: Eagle
features.description: While raging, you have a flying speed equal to your current walking speed. This benefit works only in short bursts; you fall if you end your turn in the air and nothing else is holding you aloft.

features.name: Elk
features.description: While raging, you may use a bonus action during your move to pass through the space of a Large or smaller creature. That creature must pass a Strength saving throw (DC 8 + your Strength bonus + your proficiency bonus) or be knocked prone and take bludgeoning damage equal to 1d12 + your Strength modifier.

features.name: Tiger
features.description: While you're raging, if you move at least 20 feet in a straight line toward a Large or smaller target right before making a melee weapon attack against it, you may use a bonus action to make an additional melee weapon attack against it.

features.name: Totemic Attunement
features.description: Grants the listed option features for this subclass feature at this level.

features.name: Wolf
features.description: While you're raging, you may use a bonus action on your turn to knock a Large or smaller creature prone when you hit it with melee weapon attack.

subclasses.name: Ancestral Guardian
subclasses.description: Your rage is the combination of all your ancestors.

features.name: Ancestral Protectors
features.description: Spectral warriors appear when you enter your rage. While you're raging, the first creature you hit with an attack on your turn becomes the target of the warriors, which hinder its attacks.

features.name: Spirit Shield
features.description: If you are raging and another creature you may see within 30 feet of you takes damage, you may use your reaction to reduce that damage by 2d6. When you reach certain levels in this class, you may reduce the damage by more: by 3d6 at 10th level and by 4d6 at 14th level.

features.name: Consult the Spirits
features.description: You receive the ability to consult with your ancestral spirits. When you do so, you cast the augury or clairvoyance spell, without using a spell slot or material components. Rather than creating a spherical sensor, this use of clairvoyance invisibly summons one of your ancestral spirits to the chosen location.

features.name: Vengeful Ancestors
features.description: When you use your Spirit Shield to reduce the damage of an attack, the attacker takes an amount of force damage equal to the damage that your Spirit Shield prevents.

subclasses.name: Storm Herald
subclasses.description: Your rage is second only to that of Mother Nature, who joins you.

features.name: Desert
features.description: When this effect is activated, all other creatures in your aura take 2 fire damage each. The damage increases when you reach certain levels in this class, increasing to 3 at 5th level, 4 at 10th level, 5 at 15th level, and 6 at 20th level.

features.name: Sea
features.description: When this effect is activated, you may choose one other creature you may see in your aura. The target makes a Dexterity saving throw. The target takes 1d6 lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

features.name: Storm Aura
features.description: You emanate a stormy, magical aura while you rage. The aura extends 10 feet from you in every direction, but not through Cover. Your aura has an effect that activates when you enter your rage, and you may activate the effect again on each of your turns as a bonus action.

features.name: Tundra
features.description: When this effect is activated, each creature of your choice in your aura gains 2 temporary hit points, as icy spirits inure it to suffering. The temporary hit points increase when you reach certain levels in this class, increasing to 3 at 5th level, 4 at 10th level, 5 at 15th level, and 6 at 20th level.

features.name: Desert
features.description: You receive resistance to fire damage, and you don't suffer the effects of extreme heat, as described in the Dungeon Master's Guide. Moreover, as an action, you may touch a flammable object that isn't being worn or carried by anyone else and set it on fire.

features.name: Sea
features.description: You receive resistance to lightning damage, and you may breathe underwater. You also gain a swimming speed of 30 feet.

features.name: Storm Soul
features.description: The storm grants you benefits even when your aura isn't active.

features.name: Tundra
features.description: You receive resistance to cold damage, and you don't suffer the effects of extreme cold, as described in the Dungeon Master's Guide. Moreover, as an action, you may touch water and turn a 5-foot cube of it into ice, which melts after 1 minute. This action fails if a creature is in the cube.

features.name: Shielding Storm
features.description: You learn to use your mastery of the storm to protect others. Each creature of your choice has the damage resistance you gained from the Storm Soul feature while the creature is in your Storm Aura.

features.name: Desert
features.description: Immediately after a creature in your aura hits you with an attack, you may use your reaction to force that creature to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes fire damage equal to half your barbarian level.

features.name: Raging Storm
features.description: Grants the listed option features for this subclass feature at this level.

features.name: Sea
features.description: When you hit a creature in your aura with an attack, you may use your reaction to force that creature to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is knocked prone, as if struck by a wave.

features.name: Tundra
features.description: Whenever the effect of your Storm Aura is activated, you may choose one creature you may see in the aura. That creature must pass a Strength saving throw, or its speed is reduced to 0 until the start of your next turn, as magical frost covers it.

subclasses.name: Zealot
subclasses.description: The rage acts as a gift from the gods. You are their champion.

features.name: Divine Fury
features.description: While you're raging, the first creature you hit on each of your turns with a weapon attack takes extra damage equal to 1d6 + half your barbarian level. The extra damage is necrotic or radiant; you choose the type of damage when you receive this feature.

features.name: Warrior of the Gods
features.description: If a spell, such as raise dead, has the sole effect of restoring you to life (but not undeath), the caster doesn't need material components to cast the spell on you.

features.name: Fanatical Focus
features.description: The divine power that fuels your rage can protect you. If you fail a saving throw while you're raging, you may reroll it, and you must use the new roll. You may use this ability only once per rage.

features.name: Zealous Presence
features.description: You learn to channel divine power to inspire zealotry in others. As a bonus action, you unleash a battle cry infused with divine energy. Up to ten other creatures of your choice within 60 feet of you that can hear you receive advantage on attack rolls and saving throws until the start of your next turn.

features.name: Rage beyond Death
features.description: The divine power that fuels your rage allows you to shrug off fatal blows. While you're raging, having 0 hit points doesn't knock you unconscious. You still makes death saving throws, and you suffer the normal effects of taking damage while at 0 hit points.

subclasses.name: Beast
subclasses.description: Gifts from the beasts of this world as you manifest their power.

features.name: Form of the Beast
features.description: When you enter your rage, you may transform, revealing the bestial power within you. Until the rage ends, you manifest a natural weapon. It counts as a simple melee weapon for you, and you add your Strength modifier to the attack and damage rolls when you attack with it, as normal.

features.name: Bestial Soul
features.description: The feral power within you increases, causing the natural weapons of your Form of the Beast to count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

features.name: Infectious Fury
features.description: When you hit a creature with your natural weapons while you are raging, the beast within you may curse your target with rabid fury. The target must pass a Wisdom saving throw (DC equal to 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus) or suffer one of the following effects (your choice): The target must use its reaction to make a melee attack against another creature of your choice that you may see.

features.name: Call the Hunt
features.description: When you enter your rage, you may choose a number of other willing creatures you may see within 30 feet of you equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of one creature). You receive 5 temporary hit points for each creature that accepts this feature.

subclasses.name: Wild Magic
subclasses.description: Magic suffuses your rage, producing unpredictable surges and empowering you or your allies.

features.name: Magic Awareness
features.description: As an action, sense the location of spells and magic items within 60 feet that are not behind total cover, and learn the school of any spell sensed. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus and regain all uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Wild Surge
features.description: When you enter your rage, roll on the Wild Magic table and produce the resulting effect. If the effect requires a saving throw, its DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier.

features.name: Bolstering Magic
features.description: As an action, touch a creature and grant it either a d3 bonus to attack rolls and ability checks for 10 minutes or a rolled spell-slot recovery. You can use this action a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus and regain all uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Unstable Backlash
features.description: Immediately after taking damage or failing a saving throw while raging, use your reaction to roll on the Wild Magic table and replace your current Wild Magic effect with the new one.

features.name: Controlled Surge
features.description: Whenever you roll on the Wild Magic table, roll twice and choose which effect to unleash. If both rolls match, choose any effect on the table.

subclasses.name: Battlerager
subclasses.description: Giving yourself over to your rage, make your body a weapon

features.name: Battlerager Armor
features.description: When you choose this path at 3rd level, you receive the ability to use spiked armor as a weapon. While you are wearing spiked armor and are raging, you may use a bonus action to make one melee weapon attack with your armor spikes at a target within 5 feet of you. If the attack hits, the spikes deal 1d4 piercing damage.

features.name: Restriction-Dwarves Only
features.description: Applies the setting-specific character restriction described for this subclass.

features.name: Reckless Abandon
features.description: When you use Reckless Attack while raging, you also gain temporary hit points equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1). They vanish if any of them are left when your rage ends.

features.name: Battlerager Charge
features.description: You may take the Dash action as a bonus action while you are raging.

features.name: Spiked Retribution
features.description: When a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with a melee attack, the attacker takes 3 piercing damage if you are raging, aren't incapacitated, and are wearing spiked armor.

name: Bard
description: A poet, a singer, a storyteller. There are those that seek to bring wonder to the world, and their magic comes from their emotions from the stories they tell and the songs they sing. Bards have different colleges that they follow to hone their skills in magic.

subclasses.name: College of Lore
subclasses.description: A way to keep the stories of history and civilizations alive

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: When you join the College of Lore at 3rd level, you receive proficiency with three skills of your choice.

features.name: Cutting Words
features.description: Also at 3rd level, you learn how to use your wit to distract, confuse, and otherwise sap the confidence and competence of others. When a creature that you may see within 60 feet of you makes an attack roll, an ability check, or a damage roll, you may use your reaction to expend one of your uses of Bardic Inspiration, rolling a Bardic Inspiration die and subtracting the number rolled from the creature's roll.

features.name: Additional Magical Secrets
features.description: You learn two spells of your choice from any class. A spell you choose must be of a level you may cast, as shown on the Bard table, or a cantrip. The chosen spells count as bard spells for you but don't count against the number of bard spells you know.

features.name: Peerless Skill
features.description: When you make an ability check, you may expend one use of Bardic Inspiration. Roll a Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to your ability check. You may choose to do so after you roll the die for the ability check, but before the DM tells you whether you succeed or fail.

subclasses.name: College of Valor
subclasses.description: Those that tell vibrant and powerful war stories.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: When you join the College of Valor at 3rd level, you receive proficiency with medium armor, shields, and martial weapons.

features.name: Combat Inspiration
features.description: Also at 3rd level, you learn to inspire others in battle. A creature that has a Bardic Inspiration die from you may roll that die and add the number rolled to a weapon damage roll it just made.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Battle Magic
features.description: You have mastered the art of weaving spellcasting and weapon use into a single harmonious act. When you use your action to cast a bard spell, you may make one weapon attack as a bonus action.

subclasses.name: College of Creation
subclasses.description: Your music and stories shape the very fabric of reality

features.name: Mote of Potential
features.description: Whenever you give a creature a Bardic Inspiration die, you may utter a note from the Song of Creation to create a Tiny mote of potential, which orbits within 5 feet of that creature. The mote is intangible and invulnerable, and it lasts until the Bardic Inspiration die is lost.

features.name: Performance of Creation
features.description: As an action, you may channel the magic of the Song of Creation to create one nonmagical item of your choice in an unoccupied space within 10 feet of you. The item must appear on a surface or in a liquid that can support it. The created item disappears after a number of hours equal to your proficiency bonus.

features.name: Animating Performance
features.description: As an action, you may animate one Large or smaller nonmagical item within 30 feet of you that isn't being worn or carried. The animate item uses the Dancing Item stat block, which uses your proficiency bonus (PB). It lives for 1 hour, until it is reduced to 0 hit points, or until you die.

features.name: Creative Crescendo
features.description: The number of items equals your Charisma modifier (minimum of two items). Only one of these items can be of the maximum size you may create; the rest must be Small or Tiny.

subclasses.name: College of Glamor
subclasses.description: Blessed by the Feywild, your looks are rivaled by none.

features.name: Enthralling Performance
features.description: If you perform for at least 1 minute, you may attempt to inspire wonder in your audience by singing, reciting a poem, or dancing. At the end of the performance, choose a number of humanoids within 60 feet of you who watched and listened to all of it, up to a number equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one). Each target must pass a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or be charmed by you.

features.name: Mantle of Inspiration
features.description: When you join the College of Glamour at 3rd level, you receive the ability to weave a song of fey magic that imbues your allies with vigor and speed. As a bonus action, you may expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to grant yourself a wondrous appearance.

features.name: Mantle of Majesty
features.description: You receive the ability to cloak yourself in a fey magic that makes others want to serve you. As a bonus action, you cast command, without expending a spell slot, and you take on an appearance of unearthly beauty for 1 minute or until your concentration ends (as if you were concentrating on a spell). During this time, you may cast command as a bonus action on each of your turns, without expending a spell slot.

features.name: Unbreakable Majesty
features.description: Also, as a bonus action, you may assume a magically majestic presence for 1 minute or until you are incapacitated. For the duration, whenever any creature tries to attack you for the first time on a turn, the attacker makes a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, it can't attack you on this turn, and it must choose a new target for its attack or the attack is wasted.

subclasses.name: College of Swords
subclasses.description: Become the best and most elegant sword fighter in the world.

features.name: Blade Flourish
features.description: You learn to perform impressive displays of martial prowess and speed. Whenever you take the Attack action on your turn, your walking speed increases by 10 feet until the end of the turn, and if a weapon attack that you make as part of this action hits a creature, you may use one of the following Blade Flourish options of your choice. You may use only one Blade Flourish option per turn.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: When you join the College of Swords at 3rd level, you receive proficiency with medium armor and the scimitar. If you're proficient with a simple or martial melee weapon, you may use it as a spellcasting focus for your bard spells.

features.name: Defensive Flourish
features.description: You may expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to cause the weapon to deal extra damage to the target you hit. The damage equals the number you roll on the Bardic Inspiration die. You also add the number rolled to your AC until the start of your next turn.

features.name: Fighting Style
features.description: You choose one of the fighting style options allowed by this subclass.

features.name: Mobile Flourish
features.description: You may expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to cause the weapon to deal extra damage to the target you hit. The damage equals the number you roll on the Bardic Inspiration die. You may also push the target up to 5 feet away from you, plus a number of feet equal to the number you roll on that die.

features.name: Slashing Flourish
features.description: You may expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to cause the weapon to deal extra damage to the target you hit and to any other creature of your choice that you may see within 5 feet of you. The damage equals the number you roll on the Bardic Inspiration die.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Master's Flourish
features.description: Whenever you use a Blade Flourish option, you may roll a d6 and use it instead of expending a Bardic Inspiration die.

subclasses.name: College of Whispers
subclasses.description: The hypnotic power of bards can be used for stealth

features.name: Psychic Blades
features.description: When you join the College of Whispers at 3rd level, you receive the ability to make your weapon attacks magically toxic to a creature's mind. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you may expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to deal an extra 2d6 psychic damage to that target. You may do so only once per round on your turn.

features.name: Words of Terror
features.description: You learn to infuse innocent-seeming words with an insidious magic that can inspire terror. At the end of the conversation, the target must pass a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or be frightened of you or another creature of your choice. The target is frightened in this way for 1 hour, until it is attacked or damaged, or until it witnesses its allies being attacked or damaged.

features.name: Mantle of Whispers
features.description: You receive the ability to adopt a humanoid's persona. When a humanoid dies within 30 feet of you, you may magically capture its shadow using your reaction. You retain this shadow until you use it or you finish a long rest.

features.name: Shadow Lore
features.description: You receive the ability to weave dark magic into your words and tap into a creature's deepest fears. As an action, you magically whisper a phrase that only one creature of your choice within 30 feet of you may hear. The target makes a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC.

subclasses.name: College of Eloquence
subclasses.description: Words have power; use them to shape any situation

features.name: Silver Tongue
features.description: When you make a Charisma (Persuasion) or Charisma (Deception) check, you may treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

features.name: Unsettling Words
features.description: You may spin words laced with magic that unsettle a creature and cause it to doubt itself. As a bonus action, you may expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration and choose one creature you may see within 60 feet of you. Roll the Bardic Inspiration die.

features.name: Unfailing Inspiration
features.description: When a creature adds one of your Bardic Inspiration dice to its ability check, attack roll, or saving throw and the roll fails, the creature can keep the Bardic Inspiration die.

features.name: Universal Speech
features.description: You have gained the ability to make your speech intelligible to any creature. As an action, choose one or more creatures within 60 feet of you, up to a number equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a spell slot to use it again.

features.name: Infectious Inspiration
features.description: When a creature within 60 feet of you adds one of your Bardic Inspiration dice to its ability check, attack roll, or saving throw and the roll succeeds, you may use your reaction to encourage a different creature (other than yourself) that can hear you within 60 feet of you, giving it a Bardic Inspiration die without expending any of your Bardic Inspiration uses.

subclasses.name: College of Spirits
subclasses.description: The dead tell stories and have experiences that you can draw on.

features.name: Guiding Whispers
features.description: You may reach out to spirits to guide you and others. You learn the guidance cantrip, which doesn't count against the number of bard cantrips you know. For you, it has a range of 60 feet when you cast it.

features.name: Spiritual Focus
features.description: You may use the following objects as a spellcasting focus for your bard spells: a candle, crystal ball, skull, spirit board, or tarokka deck. When you cast a bard spell that deals damage or restores hit points through the Spiritual Focus, roll a d6, and you receive a bonus to one damage or healing roll of the spell equal to the number rolled.

features.name: Tales from Beyond
features.description: You reach out to spirits who tell their tales through you. While you are holding your Spiritual Focus, you may use a bonus action to expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration and roll on the Spirit Tales table using your Bardic Inspiration die to determine the tale the spirits direct you to tell. You retain the tale in mind until you bestow the tale's effect or you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Spirit Session
features.description: You may conduct an hour-long ritual channeling spirits (which can be done during a short or long rest) using your Spiritual Focus. You may conduct the ritual with a number of willing creatures equal to your proficiency bonus (including yourself). At the end of the ritual, you temporarily learn one spell of your choice from any class.

features.name: Mystical Connection
features.description: You now have the ability to nudge the spirits of Tales from Beyond toward certain tales. Whenever you roll on the Spirit Tales table, you may roll the die twice and choose which of the two effects to bestow. If you roll the same number on both dice, you may ignore the number and choose any effect on the table.

name: Cleric
description: Ideas are not just a simple blueprint for a machine or an urge to buy new farming equipment. Ideas are immortal things that can topple empires and bring justice to those that threaten others. Ideas are some of the most powerful forces in the universe. All it needs is a champion, and clerics are those champions. Usually following a pantheon or a god/goddess, they devote their lives to serving that idea.

subclasses.name: Knowledge Domain
subclasses.description: Serve the idea that knowledge is power, and it must endure

features.name: Blessings of Knowledge
features.description: You learn two languages of your choice. You also become proficient in your choice of two of the following skills: Arcana, History, Nature, or Religion. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of those skills.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: Always prepares domain spells at the listed cleric levels: 1st: command, identify; 3rd: augury, suggestion; 5th: nondetection, speak with dead; 7th: arcane eye, confusion; 9th: legend lore, scrying.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Knowledge of the Ages
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to tap into a divine well of knowledge. As an action, you choose one skill or tool. For 10 minutes, you have proficiency with the chosen skill or tool.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Read Thoughts
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to read a creature's thoughts. You may then use your access to the creature's mind to command it. As an action, choose one creature that you may see within 60 feet of you.

features.name: Potent Spellcasting
features.description: You add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

features.name: Visions of the Past
features.description: You may meditate in this way for a number of minutes equal to your Wisdom score and must maintain concentration during that time, as if you were casting a spell. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest. After meditating for 1 minute, you learn how the owner acquired and lost the object, as well as the most recent significant event involving the object and that owner.

subclasses.name: Life Domain
subclasses.description: Serve the idea of life; every life and living thing is a wonder

features.name: Bonus Proficiency
features.description: When you choose this domain at 1st level, you receive proficiency with heavy armor.

features.name: Disciple of Life
features.description: Also starting at 1st level, your healing spells are more effective. Whenever you use a spell of 1st level or higher to restore hit points to a creature, the creature regains additional hit points equal to 2 + the spell's level.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: Always prepares domain spells at the listed cleric levels: 1st: bless, cure wounds; 3rd: lesser restoration, spiritual weapon; 5th: beacon of hope, revivify; 7th: death ward, guardian of faith; 9th: mass cure wounds, raise dead.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Preserve Life
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to heal the badly injured. As an action, you present your holy symbol and evoke healing energy that can restore a number of hit points equal to five times your cleric level. Choose any creatures within 30 feet of you, and divide those hit points among them.

features.name: Blessed Healer
features.description: The healing spells you cast on others heal you as well. When you cast a spell of 1st level or higher that restores hit points to a creature other than you, you regain hit points equal to 2 + the spell's level.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: You receive the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with divine energy. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you may cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 radiant damage to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.

features.name: Supreme Healing
features.description: When you would normally roll one or more dice to restore hit points with a spell, you instead use the highest number possible for each die. For example, instead of restoring 2d6 hit points to a creature, you restore 12.

subclasses.name: Light Domain
subclasses.description: Serve the idea of light; it will burn back the forces of shadow

features.name: Bonus Cantrip
features.description: When you choose this domain at 1st level, you receive the light cantrip if you don't already know it.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: Always prepares domain spells at the listed cleric levels: 1st: burning hands, faerie fire; 3rd: flaming sphere, scorching ray; 5th: daylight, fireball; 7th: guardian of faith, wall of fire; 9th: flame strike, scrying.

features.name: Warding Flare
features.description: Also at 1st level, you may interpose divine light between yourself and an attacking enemy. When you are attacked by a creature within 30 feet of you that you may see, you may use your reaction to impose disadvantage on the attack roll, causing light to flare before the attacker before it hits or misses. An attacker that can't be blinded is immune to this feature.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Radiance of the Dawn
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to harness sunlight, banishing darkness and dealing radiant damage to your foes. As an action, you present your holy symbol, and any magical darkness within 30 feet of you is dispelled. Also, each hostile creature within 30 feet of you makes a Constitution saving throw.

features.name: Improved Flare
features.description: You may also use your Warding Flare feature when a creature that you may see within 30 feet of you attacks a creature other than you.

features.name: Potent Spellcasting
features.description: You add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

features.name: Corona of Light
features.description: You may use your action to activate an aura of sunlight that lasts for 1 minute or until you dismiss it using another action. You emit bright light in a 60-foot radius and dim light 30 feet beyond that. Your enemies in the bright light have disadvantage on saving throws against any spell that deals fire or radiant damage.

subclasses.name: Nature Domain
subclasses.description: Serve the idea of nature; the natural world can’t ever fall.

features.name: Acolyte of Nature
features.description: You learn one druid cantrip of your choice. You also gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Animal Handling, Nature, or Survival.

features.name: Bonus Proficiency
features.description: Also at 1st level, you receive proficiency with heavy armor.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: Always prepares domain spells at the listed cleric levels: 1st: animal friendship, speak with animals; 3rd: barkskin, spike growth; 5th: plant growth, wind wall; 7th: dominate beast, grasping vine; 9th: insect plague, tree stride.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Charm Animals and Plants
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to charm animals and plants. As an action, you present your holy symbol and invoke the name of your deity. Each beast or plant creature that can see you within 30 feet of you makes a Wisdom saving throw.

features.name: Dampen Elements
features.description: When you or a creature within 30 feet of you takes acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder damage, you may use your reaction to grant resistance to the creature against that instance of the damage.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: You receive the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with divine energy. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you may cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 cold, fire, or lightning damage (your choice) to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.

features.name: Master of Nature
features.description: You receive the ability to command animals and plant creatures. While creatures are charmed by your Charm Animals and Plants feature, you may take a bonus action on your turn to verbally command what each of those creatures will do on its next turn.

subclasses.name: Tempest Domain
subclasses.description: Serve the idea of change, storms are powerful and resistant

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You receive proficiency with martial weapons and heavy armor.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: Always prepares domain spells at the listed cleric levels: 1st: fog cloud, thunderwave; 3rd: gust of wind, shatter; 5th: call lightning, sleet storm; 7th: control water, ice storm; 9th: destructive wave, insect plague.

features.name: Wrath of the Storm
features.description: Also at 1st level, you may thunderously rebuke attackers. When a creature within 5 feet of you that you may see hits you with an attack, you may use your reaction to cause the creature to make a Dexterity saving throw. The creature takes 2d8 lightning or thunder damage (your choice) on a failed saving throw, and half as much damage on a successful one.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Destructive Wrath
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to wield the power of the storm with unchecked ferocity. When you roll lightning or thunder damage, you may use your Channel Divinity to deal maximum damage, instead of rolling.

features.name: Thunderbolt Strike
features.description: When you deal lightning damage to a Large or smaller creature, you may also push it up to 10 feet away from you.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: You receive the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with divine energy. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you may cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 thunder damage to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.

features.name: Stormborn
features.description: You have a flying speed equal to your current walking speed whenever you are not underground or indoors.

subclasses.name: Trickery Domain
subclasses.description: Serve the idea of deception, pranks, or more keeps the world moving

features.name: Blessing of the Trickster
features.description: Starting when you choose this domain at 1st level, you may use your action to touch a willing creature other than yourself to give it advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks. This blessing lasts for 1 hour or until you use this feature again.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: Always prepares domain spells at the listed cleric levels: 1st: charm person, disguise self; 3rd: mirror image, pass without trace; 5th: blink, dispel magic; 7th: dimension door, polymorph; 9th: dominate person, modify memory.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Invoke Duplicity
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to create an illusory duplicate of yourself. As an action, you create a perfect illusion of yourself that lasts for 1 minute, or until you lose your concentration (as if you were concentrating on a spell). The illusion appears in an unoccupied space that you may see within 30 feet of you.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Cloak of Shadows
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to vanish. As an action, you become invisible until the end of your next turn. You become visible if you attack or cast a spell.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: You receive the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with poison-a gift from your deity. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you may cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 poison damage to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.

features.name: Improved Duplicity
features.description: You may create up to four duplicates of yourself, instead of one, when you use Invoke Duplicity. As a bonus action on your turn, you may move any number of them up to 30 feet, to a maximum range of 120 feet.

subclasses.name: War Domain
subclasses.description: Serve the idea of war, whether it’s war for honor or power.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You receive proficiency with martial weapons and heavy armor.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: Always prepares domain spells at the listed cleric levels: 1st: divine favor, shield of faith; 3rd: magic weapon, spiritual weapon; 5th: crusader's mantle, spirit guardians; 7th: freedom of movement, stoneskin; 9th: flame strike, hold monster.

features.name: War Priest
features.description: When you use the Attack action, you may make one weapon attack as a bonus action. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Guided Strike
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to strike with supernatural accuracy. When you make an attack roll, you may use your Channel Divinity to gain a +10 bonus to the roll. You make this choice after you see the roll, but before the DM says whether the attack hits or misses.

features.name: Channel Divinity: War God's Blessing
features.description: When a creature within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll, you may use your reaction to grant that creature a +10 bonus to the roll, using your Channel Divinity. You make this choice after you see the roll, but before the DM says whether the attack hits or misses.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: You receive the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with divine energy. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you may cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 damage of the same type dealt by the weapon to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.

features.name: Avatar of Battle
features.description: You receive resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks.

subclasses.name: Death Domain
subclasses.description: Serve the idea of death; everything must eventually end.

features.name: Bonus Proficiency
features.description: When the cleric chooses this domain at 1st level, he or she gains proficiency with martial weapons.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: Always prepares domain spells at the listed cleric levels: 1st: false life, ray of sickness; 3rd: blindness/deafness, ray of enfeeblement; 5th: animate dead, vampiric touch; 7th: blight, death ward; 9th: antilife shell, cloudkill.

features.name: Reaper
features.description: The cleric learns one necromancy cantrip of his or her choice from any spell list. When the cleric casts a necromancy cantrip that normally targets only one creature, the spell can instead target two creatures within range and within 5 feet of each other.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Touch of Death
features.description: The cleric can use Channel Divinity to destroy another creature's life force by touch. When the cleric hits a creature with a melee attack, the cleric can use Channel Divinity to deal extra necrotic damage to the target. The damage equals 5 + twice his or her cleric level.

features.name: Inescapable Destruction
features.description: The cleric's ability to channel negative energy becomes more potent. Necrotic damage dealt by the character's cleric spells and Channel Divinity options ignores resistance to necrotic damage.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: The cleric gains the ability to infuse his or her weapon strikes with necrotic energy. Once on each of the cleric's turns when he or she hits a creature with a weapon attack, the cleric can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 necrotic damage to the target. When the cleric reaches 14th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.

features.name: Improved Reaper
features.description: When the cleric casts a Necromancy spell of 1st through 5th-level that targets only one creature, the spell can instead target two creatures within range and within 5 feet of each other. If the spell consumes its material components, the cleric must provide them for each target.

subclasses.name: Twilight Domain
subclasses.description: Serve the idea of balance; those that attempt to disrupt it must be stopped

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You receive proficiency with martial weapons and heavy armor.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: Always prepares domain spells at the listed cleric levels: 1st: faerie fire, sleep; 3rd: moonbeam, see invisibility; 5th: aura of vitality, Leomund's tiny hut; 7th: aura of life, greater invisibility; 9th: circle of power, mislead.

features.name: Eyes of Night
features.description: You have darkvision out to a range of 300 feet. As an action, you may magically share the darkvision of this feature with willing creatures you may see within 10 feet of you, up to a number of creatures equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of one creature). Once you share it, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a spell slot of any level to share it again.

features.name: Vigilant Blessing
features.description: As an action, you give one creature you touch (including possibly yourself) advantage on the next initiative roll the creature makes. This benefit ends immediately after the roll or if you use this feature again.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Twilight Sanctuary
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to refresh your allies with soothing twilight. As an action, you present your holy symbol, and a sphere of twilight emanates from you. The sphere moves with you, and it lasts for 1 minute or until you are incapacitated or die.

features.name: Steps of Night
features.description: As a bonus action when you are in dim light or darkness, you may magically give yourself a flying speed equal to your walking speed for 1 minute. You may use this bonus action a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: You receive the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with divine energy. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you may cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 radiant damage. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.

features.name: Twilight Shroud
features.description: The twilight that you summon offers a protective embrace: you and your allies have Cover while in the sphere created by your Twilight Sanctuary.

subclasses.name: Order Domain
subclasses.description: Serve the idea of order and law; you are the voice of justice.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: When you choose this domain at 1st level, you receive proficiency with heavy armor. You also gain proficiency in the Intimidation or Persuasion skill (your choice).

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: Always prepares domain spells at the listed cleric levels: 1st: command, heroism; 3rd: hold person, zone of truth; 5th: mass healing word, slow; 7th: compulsion, locate creature; 9th: commune, dominate person.

features.name: Voice of Authority
features.description: You may invoke the power of law to drive an ally to attack. If you cast a spell with a spell slot of 1st level or higher and target an ally with the spell, that ally can use their reaction immediately after the spell to make one weapon attack against a creature of your choice that you may see. If the spell targets more than one ally, you choose the ally who can make the attack.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Order's Demand
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to exert an intimidating presence over others. As an action, you present your holy symbol, and each creature of your choice that can see or hear you within 30 feet of you must pass a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you until the end of your next turn or until the charmed creature takes any damage.

features.name: Embodiment of the Law
features.description: If you cast a spell of the enchantment school using a spell slot of 1st level or higher, you may change the spell's casting time to 1 bonus action for this casting, provided the spell's casting time is normally 1 action. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: You receive the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with divine energy. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you may cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 psychic damage to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.

features.name: Order's Wrath
features.description: If you deal your Divine Strike damage to a creature on your turn, you may curse that creature until the start of your next turn. The next time one of your allies hits the cursed creature with an attack, the target also takes 2d8 psychic damage, and the curse ends. You may curse a creature in this way only once per turn.

subclasses.name: Forge Domain
subclasses.description: Serve the idea of creation; the forges you touch will never fail

features.name: Blessing of the Forge
features.description: You receive the ability to imbue magic into a weapon or armor. At the end of a long rest, you may touch one nonmagical object that is a suit of armor or a simple or martial weapon. Until the end of your next long rest or until you die, the object becomes a magic item, granting a +1 bonus to AC if it's armor or a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls if it's a weapon.

features.name: Bonus Proficiency
features.description: When you choose this domain at 1st level, you receive proficiency with heavy armor and smith's tools.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: Always prepares domain spells at the listed cleric levels: 1st: identify, searing smite; 3rd: heat metal, magic weapon; 5th: elemental weapon, protection from energy; 7th: fabricate, wall of fire; 9th: animate objects, creation.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Artisan's Blessing
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to create simple items. The creation is completed at the end of the hour, coalescing in an unoccupied space of your choice on a surface within 5 feet of you.

features.name: Soul of the Forge
features.description: Your mastery of the forge grants you special abilities: You receive resistance to fire damage. While wearing heavy armor, you receive a +1 bonus to AC.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: You receive the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with the fiery power of the forge. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you may cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 fire damage to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.

features.name: Saint of Forge and Fire
features.description: Your blessed affinity with fire and metal becomes more powerful: You receive immunity to fire damage, While wearing heavy armor, you have resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks.

subclasses.name: Grave Domain
subclasses.description: Serve the idea of life and death; the balance must be maintained

features.name: Circle of Mortality
features.description: You receive the ability to manipulate the line between life and death. When you would normally roll one or more dice to restore hit points with a spell to a creature at 0 hit points, you instead use the highest number possible for each die. Also, you learn the spare the dying cantrip, which doesn't count against the number of cleric cantrips you know.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: Always prepares domain spells at the listed cleric levels: 1st: bane, false life; 3rd: gentle repose, ray of enfeeblement; 5th: revivify, vampiric touch; 7th: blight, death ward; 9th: antilife shell, raise dead.

features.name: Eyes of the Grave
features.description: You receive the ability to occasionally sense the presence of the undead, whose existence is an insult to the natural cycle of life. As an action, you may open your awareness to magically detect undead. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any undead within 60 feet of you that isn't behind Cover and that isn't protected from divination magic.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Path to the Grave
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to mark another creature's life force for termination. As an action, you choose one creature you may see within 30 feet of you, cursing it until the end of your next turn. The next time you or an ally of yours hits the cursed creature with an attack, the creature has vulnerability to all of that attack's damage, and then the curse ends.

features.name: Sentinel at Death's Door
features.description: You receive the ability to impede death's progress. As a reaction when you or a creature you may see within 30 feet of you suffers a critical hit, you may turn that hit into a normal hit. Any effects triggered by a critical hit are canceled.

features.name: Potent Spellcasting
features.description: You add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

features.name: Keeper of Souls
features.description: You may seize a trace of vitality from a parting soul and use it to heal the living. When an enemy you may see dies within 60 feet of you, you or one creature of your choice that is within 60 feet of you regains hit points equal to the enemy's number of Hit Dice. You may use this feature only if you aren't incapacitated.

subclasses.name: Peace Domain
subclasses.description: Serve the idea of peace; violence is almost never the answer.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: Always prepares domain spells at the listed cleric levels: 1st: heroism, sanctuary; 3rd: aid, warding bond; 5th: beacon of hope, sending; 7th: aura of purity, Otiluke's resilient sphere; 9th: greater restoration, Rary's telepathic bond.

features.name: Emboldening Bond
features.description: You may forge an empowering bond among people who are at peace with one another. As an action, you choose a number of willing creatures within 30 feet of you (this can include yourself) equal to your proficiency bonus. You create a magical bond among them for 10 minutes or until you use this feature again.

features.name: Implement of Peace
features.description: You receive proficiency in the Insight, Performance, or Persuasion skill (your choice).

features.name: Channel Divinity: Balm of Peace
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to make your very presence a soothing balm. As an action, you may move up to your speed, without provoking opportunity attacks, and when you move within 5 feet of any other creature during this action, you may restore a number of hit points to that creature equal to 2d6 + your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1 hit point).

features.name: Protective Bond
features.description: The bond you forge between people helps them protect each other. When a creature affected by your Emboldening Bond feature is about to take damage, a second bonded creature within 30 feet of the first can use its reaction to teleport to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the first creature. The second creature then takes all the damage instead.

features.name: Potent Spellcasting
features.description: You add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

features.name: Expansive Bond
features.description: The benefits of your Emboldening Bond and Protective Bond features now work when the creatures are within 60 feet of each other. Moreover, when a creature uses Protective Bond to take someone else's damage, the creature has resistance to that damage.

subclasses.name: Arcane Domain
subclasses.description: Serve the idea of magic; magic is a power and a wonder.

features.name: Arcane Initiate
features.description: Gain proficiency in Arcana and learn two wizard cantrips of your choice; they count as cleric cantrips for you.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Arcane Abjuration
features.description: Use Channel Divinity as an action to turn one celestial, elemental, fey, or fiend within 30 feet that can see or hear you. After 5th level, a failed save can also banish an eligible creature whose challenge rating is within the Arcane Banishment threshold.

features.name: Spell Breaker
features.description: When you restore hit points to an ally with a spell of 1st level or higher, you can also end one spell on that creature whose level is no higher than the spell slot used.

features.name: Potent Spellcasting
features.description: Add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

features.name: Arcane Mastery
features.description: Choose four wizard spells, one each of 6th, 7th, 8th, and 9th level, and add them to your always-prepared domain spell list as cleric spells.

name: Druid
description: Druids are protectors of nature. They are the embodiment of its wrath and its beauty. They have the ability to shapeshift into creatures and special skills, based on which circle they follow, which is a further representation of different parts of nature.

subclasses.name: Circle of the Land
subclasses.description: Grown within a certain biome, your power comes from there.

features.name: Bonus Cantrip
features.description: You learn one additional druid cantrip of your choice.

features.name: Circle Spells
features.description: Adds these subclass spells or spell options: hold person, spike growth, sleet storm, slow, freedom of movement, ice storm, commune with nature, cone of cold, mirror image, misty step, water breathing, water walk, control water, conjure elemental, scrying, blur, silence, create food and water, protection from energy, blight, hallucinatory terrain, insect plague, wall of stone, barkskin, spider climb, call lightning, plant growth, divination, tree stride, invisibility, pass without trace, daylight, haste, dream, lightning bolt, meld into stone, stone shape, stoneskin, passwall, darkness, Melf's acid arrow, stinking cloud, locate creature, web, gaseous form, greater invisibility, cloudkill.

features.name: Natural Recovery
features.description: During a short rest, you choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level that is equal to or less than half your druid level (rounded up), and none of the slots can be 6th level or higher. You can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Land's Stride
features.description: Moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs you no extra movement. You may also pass through nonmagical plants without being slowed by them and without taking damage from them if they have thorns, spines, or a similar hazard. Also, you have advantage on saving throws against plants that are magically created or manipulated to impede movement, such as those created by the entangle spell.

features.name: Nature's Ward
features.description: When you reach 10th level, you can't be charmed or frightened by elementals or fey, and you are immune to poison and disease.

features.name: Nature's Sanctuary
features.description: When you reach 14th level, creatures of the natural world sense your connection to nature and become hesitant to attack you. When a beast or plant creature attacks you, that creature makes a Wisdom saving throw against your druid spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature must choose a different target, or the attack automatically misses.

subclasses.name: Circle of the Moon
subclasses.description: Like a werewolf, your power is based on changing forms.

features.name: Circle Forms
features.description: The rites of your circle grant you the ability to transform into more dangerous animal forms. You may use your Wild Shape to transform into a beast with a challenge rating as high as 1 (you ignore the Max.

features.name: Combat Wild Shape
features.description: You receive the ability to use Wild Shape on your turn as a bonus action, rather than as an action. Also, while you are transformed by Wild Shape, you may use a bonus action to expend one spell slot to regain 1d8 hit points per level of the spell slot expended.

features.name: Primal Strike
features.description: Your attacks in beast form count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

features.name: Elemental Wild Shape
features.description: You may expend two uses of Wild Shape at the same time to transform into an air elemental, an earth elemental, a fire elemental, or a water elemental.

features.name: Thousand Forms
features.description: By 14th level, you have learned to use magic to alter your physical form in more subtle ways. You may cast the alter self spell at will.

subclasses.name: Circle of Dreams
subclasses.description: The Feywild’s nature has blessed you with the power to heal.

features.name: Balm of the Summer Court
features.description: As a bonus action, you may choose one creature you may see within 120 feet of you and spend a number of those dice equal to half your druid level or less. Roll the spent dice and add them together. The target regains a number of hit points equal to the total.

features.name: Hearth of Moonlight and Shadow
features.description: During a short or long rest, you may invoke the shadowy power of the Gloaming Court to help guard your respite. At the start of the rest, you touch a point in space, and an invisible, 30-foot-radius sphere of magic appears, centered on that point.

features.name: Hidden Paths
features.description: You may use the hidden, magical pathways that some fey use to traverse space in the blink of an eye. As a bonus action on your turn, you may teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you may see. Alternatively, you may use your action to teleport one willing creature you touch up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you may see.

features.name: Walker in Dreams
features.description: The magic of the Feywild grants you the ability to travel mentally or physically through dreamlands. When you finish a short rest, you may cast one of the following spells, without expending a spell slot or requiring material components: dream (with you as the messenger), scrying, or teleportation circle. This use of teleportation circle is special.

subclasses.name: Circle of the Shepherd
subclasses.description: Like a shepherd, you protect the animals of the world.

features.name: Speech of the Woods
features.description: You receive the ability to converse with beasts and many fey. You learn to speak, read, and write Sylvan. Also, beasts can understand your speech, and you receive the ability to decipher their noises and motions.

features.name: Spirit Totem
features.description: As a bonus action, you may magically summon an incorporeal spirit to a point you may see within 60 feet of you. As a bonus action, you may move the spirit up to 60 feet to a point you may see. The spirit persists for 1 minute or until you're incapacitated.

features.name: Mighty Summoner
features.description: Any beast or fey summoned or created by a spell that you cast gains the following benefits: The creature appears with more hit points than normal: 2 extra hit points per Hit Die it has. The damage from its natural weapons is considered magical for the purpose of overcoming immunity and resistance to nonmagical attacks and damage.

features.name: Guardian Spirit
features.description: When a beast or fey that you summoned or created with a spell ends its turn in your Spirit Totem aura, that creature regains a number of hit points equal to half your druid level.

features.name: Faithful Summons
features.description: If you are reduced to 0 hit points or are incapacitated against your will, you may immediately gain the benefits of conjure animals as if it were cast using a 9th-level spell slot. The conjured beasts appear within 20 feet of you. If they receive no commands from you, they protect you from harm and attack your foes.

subclasses.name: Circle of Spores
subclasses.description: Mycelium has many uses and abilities, including raising the dead

features.name: Circle Spells
features.description: Adds circle spells at the listed druid levels: 3rd: blindness/deafness, gentle repose; 5th: animate dead, gaseous form; 7th: blight, confusion; 9th: cloudkill, contagion.

features.name: Halo of Spores
features.description: When a creature you may see moves into a space within 10 feet of you or starts its turn there, you may use your reaction to deal 1d4 necrotic damage to that creature unless it succeeds on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. The necrotic damage increases to 1d6 at 6th level, 1d8 at 10th level, and 1d10 at 14th level.

features.name: Symbiotic Entity
features.description: You receive the ability to channel magic into your spores. As an action, you may expend a use of your Wild Shape feature to awaken those spores, rather than transforming into a beast form, and you receive 4 temporary hit points for each level you have in this class.

features.name: Fungal Infestation
features.description: Your spores gain the ability to infest a corpse and animate it. If a beast or a humanoid that is Small or Medium dies within 10 feet of you, you may use your reaction to animate it, causing it to stand up immediately with 1 hit point. The creature uses the zombie stat block in the Monster Manual.

features.name: Spreading Spores
features.description: You receive the ability to seed an area with deadly spores. As a bonus action while your Symbiotic Entity feature is active, you may hurl spores up to 30 feet away, where they swirl in a 10-foot cube for 1 minute. The spores disappear early if you use this feature again, if you dismiss them as a bonus action, or if your Symbiotic Entity feature is no longer active.

features.name: Fungal Body
features.description: The fungal spores in your body alter you: you can't be blinded, deafened, frightened, or poisoned, and any critical hit against you counts as a normal hit instead, unless you're incapacitated.
