subclasses.name: Circle of Stars
subclasses.description: The answer and guidance can always be found in the stars

features.name: Archer
features.description: When you activate this form, and as a bonus action on your subsequent turns while it lasts, you may make a ranged spell attack, hurling a luminous arrow that targets one creature within 60 feet of you. On a hit, the attack deals radiant damage equal to 1d8 + your Wisdom modifier.

features.name: Chalice
features.description: Whenever you cast a spell using a spell slot that restores hit points to a creature, you or another creature within 30 feet of you may regain hit points equal to 1d8 + your Wisdom modifier.

features.name: Dragon
features.description: When you make an Intelligence or a Wisdom check or a Constitution saving throw to maintain concentration on a spell, you may treat a roll of 9 or lower on the d20 as a 10.

features.name: Star Map
features.description: You've created a star chart as part of your heavenly studies. It is a Tiny object and can serve as a spellcasting focus for your druid spells. You determine its form by rolling on the Star Map table or by choosing one.

features.name: Starry Form
features.description: As a bonus action, you may expend a use of your Wild Shape feature to take on a starry form, rather than transforming into a beast. This form sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. It ends early if you dismiss it (no action required), are incapacitated, die, or use this feature again.

features.name: Cosmic Omen
features.description: Whenever you finish a long rest, you may consult your Star Map for omens. When you do so, roll a die.

features.name: Twinkling Constellations
features.description: The 1d8 of the Archer and the Chalice becomes 2d8, and while the Dragon is active, you have a flying speed of 20 feet and can hover. Moreover, at the start of each of your turns while in your Starry Form, you may change which constellation glimmers on your body.

features.name: Full of Stars
features.description: While in your Starry Form, you become partially incorporeal, giving you resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

subclasses.name: Circle of Wildfire
subclasses.description: Wildfires bring about change, ecosystems always revive.

features.name: Circle Spells
features.description: Adds circle spells at the listed druid levels: 2nd: burning hands, cure wounds; 3rd: flaming sphere, scorching ray; 5th: plant growth, revivify; 7th: aura of life, fire shield; 9th: flame strike, mass cure wounds.

features.name: Summon Wildfire Spirit
features.description: As an action, you may expend one use of your Wild Shape feature to summon your wildfire spirit, rather than assuming a beast form. The spirit appears in an unoccupied space of your choice that you may see within 30 feet of you. Each creature within 10 feet of the spirit (other than you) when it appears must pass a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC or take 2d6 fire damage.

features.name: Enhanced Bond
features.description: The bond with your wildfire spirit enhances your destructive and restorative spells. Whenever you cast a spell that deals fire damage or restores hit points while your wildfire spirit is summoned, roll a d8, and you receive a bonus equal to the number rolled to one damage or healing roll of the spell. Also, when you cast a spell with a range other than self, the spell can originate from you or your wildfire spirit.

features.name: Cauterizing Flames
features.description: You receive the ability to turn death into magical flames that can heal or incinerate. When a Small or larger creature dies within 30 feet of you or your wildfire spirit, a harmless spectral flame springs forth in the dead creature's space and flickers there for 1 minute.

features.name: Blazing Revival
features.description: The bond with your wildfire spirit can save you from death. If the spirit is within 120 feet of you when you are reduced to 0 hit points and thereby fall unconscious, you may cause the spirit to drop to 0 hit points. You then regain half your hit points and immediately rise to your feet.

name: Fighter
description: Swordsman ship, spears, shields, bows and crossbows. All these bring about images of fighters. Warriors who hone their combative ability to a deadly skill that rivals none. Some use magic to enhance their ability, while others crush magic in favor of pure muscle or acrobatic skills.

subclasses.name: Champion
subclasses.description: Hone your martial ability into an incredibly deadly skill with constant crits

features.name: Improved Critical
features.description: Beginning when you choose this archetype at 3rd level, your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20.

features.name: Remarkable Athlete
features.description: You may add half your proficiency bonus (round up) to any Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution check you make that doesn't already use your proficiency bonus. Also, when you make a running long jump, the distance you may cover increases by a number of feet equal to your Strength modifier.

features.name: Additional Fighting Style
features.description: You choose a second option from the fighter's Fighting Style feature.

features.name: Superior Critical
features.description: Your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 18-20.

features.name: Survivor
features.description: You attain the pinnacle of resilience in battle. At the start of each of your turns, you regain hit points equal to 5 + your Constitution modifier if you have no more than half of your hit points left. You don't gain this benefit if you have 0 hit points.

subclasses.name: Battle Master
subclasses.description: Use the art of war and tactics to gain advantages and command to victory

features.name: Combat Superiority
features.description: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you learn maneuvers that are fueled by special dice called superiority dice. Maneuvers You learn three maneuvers of your choice, which are listed under "Maneuvers" below. Many maneuvers enhance an attack in some way.

features.name: Maneuver Options
features.description: Gives access to Battle Master maneuvers fueled by superiority dice; maneuver choices expand as you gain fighter levels.

features.name: Maneuvers
features.description: Gives access to Battle Master maneuvers fueled by superiority dice; maneuver choices expand as you gain fighter levels.

features.name: Student of War
features.description: You receive proficiency with one type of artisan's tools of your choice.

features.name: Additional Maneuvers
features.description: You learn additional Battle Master maneuvers at this fighter level.

features.name: Additional Superiority Die
features.description: Your pool of superiority dice increases by one.

features.name: Know Your Enemy
features.description: If you spend at least 1 minute observing or interacting with another creature outside combat, you may learn certain information about its capabilities compared to your own.

features.name: Additional Maneuvers
features.description: You learn additional Battle Master maneuvers at this fighter level.

features.name: Improved Combat Superiority (d10)
features.description: Your superiority dice turn into d10s.

features.name: Additional Maneuvers
features.description: You learn additional Battle Master maneuvers at this fighter level.

features.name: Additional Superiority Die
features.description: Your pool of superiority dice increases by one.

features.name: Relentless
features.description: When you roll initiative and have no superiority dice remaining, you regain 1 superiority die.

features.name: Improved Combat Superiority (d12)
features.description: Your superiority dice turn into d12s.

subclasses.name: Eldritch Knight
subclasses.description: Learning wizard-like spells to gain the upperhand in any fight.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: Grants limited wizard spellcasting using the subclass progression, including cantrips and leveled spells with the listed school restrictions.

features.name: Weapon Bond
features.description: You learn a ritual that creates a magical bond between yourself and one weapon. You perform the ritual over the course of 1 hour, which can be done during a short rest. The weapon must be within your reach throughout the ritual, at the conclusion of which you touch the weapon and forge the bond.

features.name: War Magic
features.description: When you use your action to cast a cantrip, you may make one weapon attack as a bonus action.

features.name: Eldritch Strike
features.description: You learn how to make your weapon strikes undercut a creature's resistance to your spells. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, that creature has disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes against a spell you cast before the end of your next turn.

features.name: Arcane Charge
features.description: You receive the ability to teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you may see when you use your Action Surge. You may teleport before or after the additional action.

features.name: Improved War Magic
features.description: When you use your action to cast a spell, you may make one weapon attack as a bonus action.

subclasses.name: Arcane Archer
subclasses.description: Mix magic with your arrows to rain literal fire down upon your foes

features.name: Arcane Archer Lore
features.description: You learn magical theory or some of the secrets of nature-typical for practitioners of this elven martial tradition. You choose to gain proficiency in either the Arcana or the Nature skill, and you choose to learn either the prestidigitation or the druidcraft cantrip.

features.name: Arcane Shot
features.description: You learn to unleash special magical effects with some of your shots. When you receive this feature, you learn two Arcane Shot options of your choice (see "Arcane Shot Options" below). Once per turn when you fire an arrow from a shortbow or longbow as part of the Attack action, you may apply one of your Arcane Shot options to that arrow.

features.name: Arcane Shot Options
features.description: Lists the Arcane Shot options you can choose from; each option modifies an arrow with a magical effect.

features.name: Additional Arcane Shot Option
features.description: You learn one more Arcane Shot option.

features.name: Curving Shot
features.description: You learn how to direct an errant arrow toward a new target. When you make an attack roll with a magic arrow and miss, you may use a bonus action to reroll the attack roll against a different target within 60 feet of the original target.

features.name: Magic Arrow
features.description: You receive the ability to infuse arrows with magic. Whenever you fire a nonmagical arrow from a shortbow or longbow, you may make it magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage. The magic fades from the arrow immediately after it hits or misses its target.

features.name: Additional Arcane Shot Option
features.description: You learn one more Arcane Shot option.

features.name: Additional Arcane Shot Option
features.description: You learn one more Arcane Shot option.

features.name: Ever-Ready Shot
features.description: If you roll initiative and have no uses of Arcane Shot remaining, you regain one use of it.

features.name: Additional Arcane Shot Option
features.description: You learn one more Arcane Shot option.

subclasses.name: Cavalier
subclasses.description: You will never break; your skill, while mounted or not, can not be beaten.

features.name: Bonus Proficiency
features.description: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you receive proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Animal Handling, History, Insight, Performance, or Persuasion. Alternatively, you learn one language of your choice.

features.name: Born to the Saddle
features.description: You have advantage on saving throws made to avoid falling off your mount. If you fall off your mount and descend no more than 10 feet, you may land on your feet if you're not incapacitated. Finally, mounting or dismounting a creature costs you only 5 feet of movement, rather than half your speed.

features.name: Unwavering Mark
features.description: You may menace your foes, foiling their attacks and punishing them for harming others. When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you may mark the creature until the end of your next turn. This effect ends early if you are incapacitated or you die, or if someone else marks the creature.

features.name: Warding Maneuver
features.description: You learn to fend off strikes directed at you, your mount, or other creatures nearby. If you or a creature you may see within 5 feet of you is hit by an attack, you may roll 1d8 as a reaction if you're wielding a melee weapon or a shield. Roll the die, and add the number rolled to the target's AC against that attack.

features.name: Hold the Line
features.description: You become a master of locking down your enemies. Creatures provoke an opportunity attack from you when they move 5 feet or more while within your reach, and if you hit a creature with an opportunity attack, the target's speed is reduced to 0 until the end of the current turn.

features.name: Ferocious Charger
features.description: If you move at least 10 feet in a straight line right before attacking a creature and you hit it with the attack, that target must pass a Strength saving throw (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier) or be knocked prone. You may use this feature only once on each of your turns.

features.name: Vigilant Defender
features.description: You respond to danger with extraordinary vigilance. In combat, you get a special reaction that you may take once on every creature's turn, except your turn. You may use this special reaction only to make an opportunity attack, and you can't use it on the same turn that you take your normal reaction.

subclasses.name: Samurai
subclasses.description: Your fighting spirit will allow you to rain a hurricane of deadly blows.

features.name: Bonus Proficiency
features.description: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you receive proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: History, Insight, Performance, or Persuasion. Alternatively, you learn one language of your choice.

features.name: Fighting Spirit
features.description: As a bonus action on your turn, you may give yourself advantage on weapon attack rolls until the end of the current turn. When you do so, you also gain 5 temporary hit points. The number of temporary hit points increases when you reach certain levels in this class, increasing to 10 at 10th level and 15 at 15th level.

features.name: Elegant Courtier
features.description: Whenever you make a Charisma (Persuasion) check, you receive a bonus to the check equal to your Wisdom modifier. Your self-control also causes you to gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws. If you already have this proficiency, you instead gain proficiency in Intelligence or Charisma saving throws (your choice).

features.name: Tireless Spirit
features.description: When you roll initiative and have no uses of Fighting Spirit remaining, you regain one use.

features.name: Rapid Strike
features.description: You learn to trade accuracy for swift strikes. If you take the Attack action on your turn and have advantage on an attack roll against one of the targets, you may forgo the advantage for that roll to make an additional weapon attack against that target, as part of the same action. You may do so no more than once per turn.

features.name: Strength before Death
features.description: If you take damage that reduces you to 0 hit points and doesn't kill you outright, you may use your reaction to delay falling unconscious, and you may immediately take an extra turn, interrupting the current turn. While you have 0 hit points during that extra turn, taking damage causes death saving throw failures as normal, and three death saving throw failures can still kill you.

subclasses.name: Psi Warrior
subclasses.description: Blessed with psychic energy, your mind is sharpened just like your weapon.

features.name: Protective Field
features.description: When you or another creature you may see within 30 feet of you takes damage, you may use your reaction to expend one Psionic Energy die, roll the die, and reduce the damage taken by the number rolled plus your Intelligence modifier (minimum reduction of 1), as you create a momentary shield of telekinetic force.

features.name: Psionic Power
features.description: This energy is represented by your Psionic Energy dice, which are each a d6. You have a number of these dice equal to twice your proficiency bonus, and they fuel various psionic powers you have, which are detailed below. Some of your powers expend the Psionic Energy die they use, as specified in a power's description, and you can't use a power if it requires you to use a die when your dice are all expended.

features.name: Psionic Strike
features.description: Once on each of your turns, immediately after you hit a target within 30 feet of you with an attack and deal damage to it with a weapon, you may expend one Psionic Energy die, rolling it and dealing force damage to the target equal to the number rolled plus your Intelligence modifier.

features.name: Telekinetic Movement
features.description: As an action, you target one loose object that is Large or smaller or one willing creature, other than yourself. If you may see the target and it is within 30 feet of you, you may move it up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you may see. Either way, you may move the target horizontally, vertically, or both.

features.name: Psi-Powered Leap
features.description: As a bonus action, you may propel your body with your mind. You receive a flying speed equal to twice your walking speed until the end of the current turn. Once you take this bonus action, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest, unless you expend a Psionic Energy die to take it again.

features.name: Telekinetic Adept
features.description: You have mastered new ways to use your telekinetic abilities, detailed below.

features.name: Telekinetic Thrust
features.description: When you deal damage to a target with your Psionic Strike, you may force the target to make a Strength saving throw against a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier. If the save fails, you may knock the target prone or move it up to 10 feet in any direction horizontally.

features.name: Guarded Mind
features.description: You have resistance to psychic damage. Moreover, if you start your turn charmed or frightened, you may expend a Psionic Energy die and end every effect on yourself subjecting you to those conditions.

features.name: Bulwark of Force
features.description: You may shield yourself and others with telekinetic force. As a bonus action, you may choose creatures, which can include you, that you may see within 30 feet of you, up to a number of creatures equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of one creature). Each of the chosen creatures is protected by Cover for 1 minute or until you're incapacitated.

features.name: Telekinetic Master
features.description: Your ability to move creatures and objects with your mind is matched by few. You may cast the telekinesis spell, requiring no components, and your spellcasting ability for the spell is Intelligence. On each of your turns while you concentrate on the spell, including the turn when you cast it, you may make one attack with a weapon as a bonus action.

subclasses.name: Rune Knight
subclasses.description: Learn the ancient power of Giants and their powerful magic runes.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You receive proficiency with smith's tools, and you learn to speak, read, and write Giant.

features.name: Giant's Might
features.description: You have learned how to imbue yourself with the might of giants. As a bonus action, you magically gain the following benefits, which last for 1 minute: If you are smaller than Large, you become Large, along with anything you are wearing. If you lack the room to become Large, your size doesn't change.

features.name: Rune Carver
features.description: You may use magic runes to enhance your gear. You learn two runes of your choice, from among the runes described below, and each time you receive a level in this class, you may replace one rune you know with a different one from this feature. When you reach certain levels in this class, you learn additional runes, as shown in the Runes Known table.

features.name: Additional Rune Known
features.description: You learn one additional rune from the Rune Knight rune options.

features.name: Runic Shield
features.description: You learn to invoke your rune magic to protect your allies. When another creature you may see within 60 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you may use your reaction to force the attacker to reroll the d20 and use the new roll. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Additional Rune Known
features.description: You learn one additional rune from the Rune Knight rune options.

features.name: Great Stature
features.description: When you receive this feature, roll 3d4. You grow a number of inches in height equal to the roll. Moreover, the extra damage you deal with your Giant's Might feature increases to 1d8.

features.name: Additional Rune Known
features.description: You learn one additional rune from the Rune Knight rune options.

features.name: Master of Runes
features.description: Twice, rather than once, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Runic Juggernaut
features.description: You learn how to amplify your rune-powered transformation. As a result, the extra damage you deal with the Giant's Might feature increases to 1d10. Moreover, when you use that feature, your size can increase to Huge, and while you are that size, your reach increases by 5 feet.

subclasses.name: Echo Fighter
subclasses.description: Using magical energy, create an “echo” to fight with you.

features.name: Manifest Echo
features.description: You may use a bonus action to magically manifest an echo of yourself in an unoccupied space you may see within 15 feet of you. This echo is a magical, translucent, gray image of you that lasts until it is destroyed, until you dismiss it as a bonus action, until you manifest another echo, or until you're incapacitated. Your echo has AC 14 + your proficiency bonus, 1 hit point, and immunity to all conditions.

features.name: Unleash Incarnation
features.description: Whenever you take the Attack action, you may make one additional melee attack from the echo's position. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Echo Avatar
features.description: As an action, you may see through your echo's eyes and hear through its ears. You may sustain this effect for up to 10 minutes, and you may end it at any time (requires no action). While your echo is being used in this way, it can be up to 1,000 feet away from you without being destroyed.

features.name: Shadow Martyr
features.description: You may make your echo throw itself in front of an attack directed at another creature that you may see. Before the attack roll is made, you may use your reaction to teleport the echo to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the targeted creature. The attack roll that triggered the reaction is instead made against your echo.

features.name: Reclaim Potential
features.description: You've learned to absorb the fleeting magic of your echo. When an echo of yours is destroyed by taking damage, you may gain a number of temporary hit points equal to 2d6 + your Constitution modifier, provided you don't already have temporary hit points. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (a minimum of once).

features.name: Legion of One
features.description: Also, when you roll initiative and have no uses of your Unleash Incarnation feature left, you regain one use of that feature.

subclasses.name: Purple Dragon Knight
subclasses.description: A warrior that braves any battle through inspiration.

features.name: Rallying Cry
features.description: When you use Second Wind, choose up to three allied creatures within 60 feet that can see or hear you. Each regains hit points equal to your fighter level.

features.name: Royal Envoy
features.description: Gain Persuasion proficiency, or another listed skill if already proficient, and double your proficiency bonus on Persuasion checks.

features.name: Inspiring Surge
features.description: When you use Action Surge, choose one allied creature within 60 feet that can see or hear you. It can use its reaction to make one melee or ranged weapon attack. At 18th level, choose two allies instead of one.

features.name: Bulwark
features.description: When you use Indomitable to reroll an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw, one ally within 60 feet that failed the same save can reroll it if it can see or hear you.

features.name: Inspiring Surge improvement
features.description: Your Inspiring Surge can now choose two allied creatures instead of one.

name: Monk
description: Kung Fu, Karate, Drunken Fighting, Dr. Strange, ect. All of these are what the D&D Monks train in. Whether it’s using your fist, spirit, ki or weapons that are nothing but an extension of your will. Monks can deliver a storm of powerful melee attacks.

subclasses.name: Way of the Open Hand
subclasses.description: Use your fists and palms to annihilate your foe.

features.name: Open Hand Technique
features.description: You may manipulate your enemy's ki when you harness your own. Whenever you hit a creature with one of the attacks granted by your Flurry of Blows, you may impose one of the following effects on that target. It must pass a Dexterity saving throw or be knocked prone.

features.name: Wholeness of Body
features.description: You receive the ability to heal yourself. As an action, you may regain hit points equal to three times your monk level. You must finish a long rest before you may use this feature again.

features.name: Tranquility
features.description: You may enter a special meditation that surrounds you with an aura of peace. At the end of a long rest, you receive the effect of a sanctuary spell that lasts until the start of your next long rest (the spell can end early as normal). The saving throw DC for the spell equals 8 + your Wisdom modifier + your proficiency bonus.

features.name: Quivering Palm
features.description: You receive the ability to set up lethal vibrations in someone's body. When you hit a creature with an unarmed strike, you may spend 3 ki points to start these imperceptible vibrations, which last for a number of days equal to your monk level. The vibrations are harmless unless you use your action to end them.

subclasses.name: Way of the Shadow
subclasses.description: Usually called, ninjas and so forth. Their fists strike from the shadow

features.name: Shadow Arts
features.description: You may use your ki to duplicate the effects of certain spells. As an action, you may spend 2 ki points to cast darkness, darkvision, pass without trace, or silence, without providing material components. Also, you receive the minor illusion cantrip if you don't already know it.

features.name: Shadow Step
features.description: You receive the ability to step from one shadow into another. When you are in dim light or darkness, as a bonus action you may teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you may see that is also in dim light or darkness. You then have advantage on the first melee attack you make before the end of the turn.

features.name: Cloak of Shadows
features.description: By 11th level, you have learned to become one with the shadows. When you are in an area of dim light or darkness, you may use your action to become invisible. You remain invisible until you make an attack, cast a spell, or are in an area of bright light.

features.name: Opportunist
features.description: You may exploit a creature's momentary distraction when it is hit by an attack. Whenever a creature within 5 feet of you is hit by an attack made by a creature other than you, you may use your reaction to make a melee attack against that creature.

subclasses.name: Way of the Four Elements
subclasses.description: Control all 4 elements, earth, water, fire and wind in your ways

features.name: Disciple of the Elements
features.description: You learn magical disciplines that harness the power of the four elements. A discipline requires you to spend ki points each time you use it. You learn one additional elemental discipline of your choice at 6th, 11th, and 17th level.

features.name: Elemental Disciplines
features.description: Lists elemental disciplines you can learn and fuel with ki, including spell-like effects and elemental techniques.

features.name: Extra Elemental Discipline
features.description: You learn one additional elemental discipline.

features.name: Extra Elemental Discipline
features.description: You learn one additional elemental discipline.

features.name: Extra Elemental Discipline
features.description: You learn one additional elemental discipline.

subclasses.name: Way of Mercy
subclasses.description: Your ki and spirit is meant to heal wounds, not create them.

features.name: Hand of Harm
features.description: You use your ki to inflict wounds. When you hit a creature with an unarmed strike, you may spend 1 ki point to deal extra necrotic damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die + your Wisdom modifier. You may use this feature only once per turn.

features.name: Hand of Healing
features.description: As an action, you may spend 1 ki point to touch a creature and restore a number of hit points equal to a roll of your Martial Arts die + your Wisdom modifier. When you use your Flurry of Blows, you may replace one of the unarmed strikes with a use of this feature without spending a ki point for the healing.

features.name: Implements of Mercy
features.description: You receive proficiency in the Insight and Medicine skills, and you receive proficiency with the herbalism kit. You determine its appearance, or generate it randomly by rolling on the Merciful Mask table. Merciful Mask; 1: Raven; 2: Blank and white; 3: Crying visage; 4: Laughing visage; 5: Skull; 6: Butterfly

features.name: Physician's Touch
features.description: You may administer even greater cures with a touch, and if you feel it's necessary, you may use your knowledge to cause harm. When you use Hand of Healing on a creature, you may also end one disease or one of the following conditions affecting the creature: blinded, deafened, paralyzed, poisoned, or stunned.

features.name: Flurry of Healing and Harm
features.description: When you use Flurry of Blows, you may now replace each of the unarmed strikes with a use of your Hand of Healing, without spending ki points for the healing. Also, when you make an unarmed strike with Flurry of Blows, you may use Hand of Harm with that strike without spending the ki point for Hand of Harm. You may still use Hand of Harm only once per turn.

features.name: Hand of Ultimate Mercy
features.description: As an action, you may touch the corpse of a creature that died within the past 24 hours and expend 5 ki points. The creature then returns to life, regaining a number of hit points equal to 4d10 + your Wisdom modifier. If the creature died while subject to any of the following conditions, it revives with them removed: blinded, deafened, paralyzed, poisoned, and stunned.

subclasses.name: Way of the Astral Self
subclasses.description: Your spirit becomes an entity around you to help you.

features.name: Arms of the Astral Self
features.description: Your mastery of your ki allows you to summon a portion of your astral self. As a bonus action, you may spend 1 ki point to summon the arms of your astral self. When you do so, each creature of your choice that you may see within 10 feet of you must pass a Dexterity saving throw or take force damage equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die.

features.name: Forms of Your Astral Self
features.description: Provides appearance or style guidance for the subclass; it has no standalone rules effect.

features.name: Visage of the Astral Self
features.description: As a bonus action, or as part of the bonus action you take to activate Arms of the Astral Self, you may spend 1 ki point to summon this visage for 10 minutes. It vanishes early if you are incapacitated or die. The spectral visage covers your face like a helmet or mask.

features.name: Body of the Astral Self
features.description: When you have both your astral arms and visage summoned, you may cause the body of your astral self to appear (no action required). Deflect Energy When you take acid, cold, fire, force, lightning, or thunder damage, you may use your reaction to deflect it. When you do so, the damage you take is reduced by 1d10 + your Wisdom modifier (minimum reduction of 1).

features.name: Awakened Astral Self
features.description: As a bonus action, you may spend 5 ki points to summon the arms, visage, and body of your astral self and awaken it for 10 minutes. This awakening ends early if you are incapacitated or die. Armor of the Spirit You receive a +2 bonus to Armor Class.

subclasses.name: Way of the Drunken Master
subclasses.description: Confuse your foes with unpredictable attacks.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: When you choose this tradition at 3rd level, you receive proficiency in the Performance skill if you don't already have it. You also gain proficiency with brewer's supplies if you don't already have it.

features.name: Drunken Technique
features.description: You learn how to twist and turn quickly as part of your Flurry of Blows. Whenever you use Flurry of Blows, you receive the benefit of the Disengage action, and your walking speed increases by 10 feet until the end of the current turn.

features.name: Leap to Your Feet
features.description: When you're prone, you may stand up by spending 5 feet of movement, rather than half your speed.

features.name: Redirect Attack
features.description: When a creature misses you with a melee attack roll, you may spend 1 ki point as a reaction to cause that attack to hit one creature of your choice, other than the attacker, that you may see within 5 feet of you.

features.name: Tipsy Sway
features.description: Grants the listed option features for this subclass feature at this level.

features.name: Drunkard's Luck
features.description: When you make an ability check, an attack roll, or a saving throw and have disadvantage on the roll, you may spend 2 ki points to cancel the disadvantage for that roll.

features.name: Intoxicated Frenzy
features.description: You receive the ability to make an overwhelming number of attacks against a group of enemies. When you use your Flurry of Blows, you may make up to three additional attacks with it (up to a total of five Flurry of Blows attacks), provided that each Flurry of Blows attack targets a different creature this turn.

subclasses.name: Way of the Kensei
subclasses.description: Your weapons become an extension of yourself. A form of art

features.name: Agile Parry
features.description: If you make an unarmed strike as part of the Attack action on your turn and are holding a kensei weapon, you may use it to defend yourself if it is a melee weapon. You receive a +2 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn, while the weapon is in your hand and you aren't incapacitated.

features.name: Kensei's Shot
features.description: You may use a bonus action on your turn to make your ranged attacks with a kensei weapon more deadly. When you do so, any target you hit with a ranged attack using a kensei weapon takes an extra 1d4 damage of the weapon's type. You retain this benefit until the end of the current turn.

features.name: Path of the Kensei
features.description: When you choose this tradition at 3rd level, your special martial arts training leads you to master the use of certain weapons. Each of these weapons can be any simple or martial weapon that lacks the heavy and special properties. You receive proficiency with these weapons if you don't already have it.

features.name: One with the Blade
features.description: You extend your ki into your kensei weapons, granting you the following benefits. Magic Kensei Weapons Your attacks with your kensei weapons count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage. Deft Strike When you hit a target with a kensei weapon, you may spend 1 ki point to cause the weapon to deal extra damage to the target equal to your Martial Arts die.

features.name: Sharpen the Blade
features.description: You receive the ability to augment your weapons further with your ki. As a bonus action, you may expend up to 3 ki points to grant one kensei weapon you touch a bonus to attack and damage rolls when you attack with it. The bonus equals the number of ki points you spent.

features.name: Unerring Accuracy
features.description: Your mastery of weapons grants you extraordinary accuracy. If you miss with an attack roll using a monk weapon on your turn, you may reroll it. You may use this feature only once on each of your turns.

subclasses.name: Way of the Sun Soul
subclasses.description: Your soul and will is so powerful that it can ignite in fire.

features.name: Radiant Sun Bolt
features.description: You receive a new attack option that you may use with the Attack action. The special attack is a ranged spell attack with a range of 30 feet. You are proficient with it, and you add your Dexterity modifier to its attack and damage rolls.

features.name: Searing Arc Strike
features.description: You receive the ability to channel your ki into searing waves of energy. Immediately after you take the Attack action on your turn, you may spend 2 ki points to cast the burning hands spell as a bonus action. You may spend additional ki points to cast burning hands as a higher-level spell.

features.name: Searing Sunburst
features.description: You receive the ability to create an orb of light that erupts into a devastating explosion. As an action, you magically create an orb and hurl it at a point you choose within 150 feet, where it erupts into a sphere of radiant light for a brief but deadly instant. Each creature in that 20-foot-radius sphere must pass a Constitution saving throw or take 2d6 radiant damage.

features.name: Sun Shield
features.description: You shed bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. You may extinguish or restore the light as a bonus action. If a creature hits you with a melee attack while this light shines, you may use your reaction to deal radiant damage to the creature.

subclasses.name: Way of Long Death
subclasses.description: Your soul and fists become the tools of death

features.name: Touch of Death
features.description: Starting when you choose this tradition at 3rd level, your study of death allows you to extract vitality from another creature as it nears its demise. When you reduce a creature within 5 feet of you to 0 hit points, you receive temporary hit points equal to your Wisdom modifier + your monk level (minimum of 1 temporary hit point).

features.name: Hour of Reaping
features.description: You receive the ability to unsettle or terrify those around you as an action, for your soul has been touched by the shadow of death. When you take this action, each creature within 30 feet of you that can see you must pass a Wisdom saving throw or be frightened of you until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Mastery of Death
features.description: You use your familiarity with death to escape its grasp. When you are reduced to 0 hit points, you may expend 1 ki point (no action required) to have 1 hit point instead.

features.name: Touch of the Long Death
features.description: As an action, you touch one creature within 5 feet of you, and you expend 1 to 10 ki points. The target makes a Constitution saving throw, and it takes 2d10 necrotic damage per ki point spent on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

subclasses.name: Way of the Ascendant Dragon
subclasses.description: Following the way of dragons, your spirit manifests their power

features.name: Breath of the Dragon
features.description: When you take the Attack action on your turn, you may replace one of the attacks with an exhalation of draconic energy in either a 20-foot cone or a 30-foot line that is 5 feet wide (your choice). Choose a damage type: acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison.

features.name: Draconic Disciple
features.description: You may channel draconic power to magnify your presence and imbue your unarmed strikes with the essence of a dragon's breath. You receive the following benefits: Draconic Presence: If you fail a Charisma (Intimidation) or Charisma (Persuasion) check, you may use your reaction to reroll the check, as you tap into the mighty presence of dragons.

features.name: Wings Unfurled
features.description: When you use your Step of the Wind, you may unfurl spectral draconic wings from your back that vanish at the end of your turn. While the wings exist, you have a flying speed equal to your walking speed. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Aspect of the Wyrm
features.description: The power of your draconic spirit now radiates from you, warding your allies or inspiring fear in your enemies. As a bonus action, you may create an aura of draconic power that radiates 10 feet from you for 1 minute.

features.name: Ascendant Aspect
features.description: Your draconic spirit reaches its peak. You receive the following benefits: Augment Breath: When you use your Breath of the Dragon, you may spend 1 ki point to augment its shape and power.

name: Paladin
description: Paladins, much like clerics, hold up ideas. However, they serve tenants with a multitude of ideas. Some of joy, others of light and conquest. Paladin holds an Oath which is the representation of said ideas.

subclasses.name: Oath of Devotion
subclasses.description: Light, Lawful, Honesty, the pure holy warrior

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: Grants the Channel Divinity options listed for this oath or domain.

features.name: Oath Spells
features.description: Always prepares oath spells at the listed paladin levels: 3rd: protection from evil and good, sanctuary; 5th: lesser restoration, zone of truth; 9th: beacon of hope, dispel magic; 13th: freedom of movement, guardian of faith; 17th: commune, flame strike.

features.name: Sacred Weapon
features.description: As an action, you may imbue one weapon that you are holding with positive energy, using your Channel Divinity. For 1 minute, you add your Charisma modifier to attack rolls made with that weapon (with a minimum bonus of +1). The weapon also emits bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light 20 feet beyond that.

features.name: Tenets of Devotion
features.description: Defines the oath's ideals and obligations; it does not add a separate combat mechanic.

features.name: Turn the Unholy
features.description: As an action, you present your holy symbol and speak a prayer censuring fiends and undead, using your Channel Divinity. Each fiend or undead that can see or hear you within 30 feet of you makes a Wisdom saving throw. If the creature fails its saving throw, it is turned for 1 minute or until it takes damage.

features.name: Aura of Devotion
features.description: You and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you can't be charmed while you are conscious. The range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

features.name: Purity of Spirit
features.description: You are always under the effects of a protection from evil and good spell.

features.name: Holy Nimbus
features.description: As an action, you may emanate an aura of sunlight. For 1 minute, bright light shines from you in a 30-foot radius, and dim light shines 30 feet beyond that. Whenever an enemy creature starts its turn in the bright light, the creature takes 10 radiant damage.

subclasses.name: Oath of the Ancients
subclasses.description: Joy and love, the ancients blessed this warrior with nature

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: Grants the Channel Divinity options listed for this oath or domain.

features.name: Nature's Wrath
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to invoke primeval forces to ensnare a foe. As an action, you may cause spectral vines to spring up and reach for a creature within 10 feet of you that you may see. The creature must pass a Strength or Dexterity saving throw (its choice) or be restrained.

features.name: Oath Spells
features.description: Always prepares oath spells at the listed paladin levels: 3rd: ensnaring strike, speak with animals; 5th: moonbeam, misty step; 9th: plant growth, protection from energy; 13th: ice storm, stoneskin; 17th: commune with nature, tree stride.

features.name: Tenets of the Ancients
features.description: Defines the oath's ideals and obligations; it does not add a separate combat mechanic.

features.name: Turn the Faithless
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to utter ancient words that are painful for fey and fiends to hear. As an action, you present your holy symbol, and each fey or fiend within 30 feet of you that can hear you makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is turned for 1 minute or until it takes damage.

features.name: Aura of Warding
features.description: You and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you have resistance to damage from spells. The range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

features.name: Undying Sentinel
features.description: When you are reduced to 0 hit points and are not killed outright, you may choose to drop to 1 hit point instead. Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest. Also, you suffer none of the drawbacks of old age, and you can't be aged magically.

features.name: Elder Champion
features.description: You may assume the form of an ancient force of nature, taking on an appearance you choose. For example, your skin might turn green or take on a bark-like texture, your hair might become leafy or moss-like, or you might sprout antlers or a lion-like mane. Using your action, you undergo a transformation.

subclasses.name: Oath of Vengeance
subclasses.description: Anger and vengeance, something bad pushed this warrior to seek vengeance

features.name: Abjure Enemy
features.description: As an action, you present your holy symbol and speak a prayer of denunciation, using your Channel Divinity. Choose one creature within 60 feet of you that you may see. That creature makes a Wisdom saving throw, unless it is immune to being frightened.

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: Grants the Channel Divinity options listed for this oath or domain.

features.name: Oath Spells
features.description: Always prepares oath spells at the listed paladin levels: 3rd: bane, hunter's mark; 5th: hold person, misty step; 9th: haste, protection from energy; 13th: banishment, dimension door; 17th: hold monster, scrying.

features.name: Tenets of Vengeance
features.description: Defines the oath's ideals and obligations; it does not add a separate combat mechanic.

features.name: Vow of Enmity
features.description: As a bonus action, you may utter a vow of enmity against a creature you may see within 10 feet of you, using your Channel Divinity. You receive advantage on attack rolls against the creature for 1 minute or until it drops to 0 hit points or falls unconscious.

features.name: Relentless Avenger
features.description: When you hit a creature with an opportunity attack, you may move up to half your speed immediately after the attack and as part of the same reaction. This movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.

features.name: Soul of Vengeance
features.description: When a creature under the effect of your Vow of Enmity makes an attack, you may use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature if it is within range.

features.name: Avenging Angel
features.description: Using your action, you undergo a transformation. For 1 hour, you receive the following benefits: Wings sprout from your back and grant you a flying speed of 60 feet. You emanate an aura of menace in a 30-foot radius.

subclasses.name: Oathbreaker
subclasses.description: They broke an oath long ago and are cursed for it

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: Grants the Channel Divinity options listed for this oath or domain.

features.name: Control Undead
features.description: As an action, the paladin targets one undead creature he or she can see within 30 feet of him or her. The target makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target must obey the paladin's commands for the next 24 hours, or until the paladin uses this Channel Divinity option again.

features.name: Dreadful Aspect
features.description: As an action, the paladin channels the darkest emotions and focuses them into a burst of magical menace. Each creature of the paladin's choice within 30 feet of the paladin makes a Wisdom saving throw if it can see the paladin. On a failed save, the target is frightened of the paladin for 1 minute.

features.name: Oathbreaker Spells
features.description: Always prepares oath spells at the listed paladin levels: 3rd: hellish rebuke, inflict wounds; 5th: crown of madness, darkness; 9th: animate dead, bestow curse; 13th: blight, confusion; 17th: contagion, dominate person.

features.name: Aura of Hate
features.description: The paladin, as well any fiends and undead within 10 feet of the paladin, gains a bonus to melee weapon damage rolls equal to the paladin's Charisma modifier (minimum of +1). The range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

features.name: Supernatural Resistance
features.description: The paladin gains resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical weapons.

features.name: Dread Lord
features.description: The paladin can, as an action, surround himself or herself with an aura of gloom that lasts for 1 minute. Whenever an enemy that is frightened by the paladin starts its turn in the aura, it takes 4d10 psychic damage. Also, the paladin and creatures he or she chooses in the aura are draped in deeper shadow.

subclasses.name: Oath of Conquest
subclasses.description: Destruction and Victory, nothing will stop this warrior.

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: Grants the Channel Divinity options listed for this oath or domain.

features.name: Conquering Presence
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to exude a terrifying presence. As an action, you force each creature of your choice that you may see within 30 feet of you to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a creature becomes frightened of you for 1 minute.

features.name: Guided Strike
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to strike with supernatural accuracy. When you make an attack roll, you may use your Channel Divinity to gain a +10 bonus to the roll. You make this choice after you see the roll, but before the DM says whether the attack hits or misses.

features.name: Oath Spells
features.description: Always prepares oath spells at the listed paladin levels: 3rd: armor of Agathys, command; 5th: hold person, spiritual weapon; 9th: bestow curse, fear; 13th: dominate beast, stoneskin; 17th: cloudkill, dominate person.

features.name: Tenets of Conquest
features.description: Defines the oath's ideals and obligations; it does not add a separate combat mechanic.

features.name: Aura of Conquest
features.description: You constantly emanate a menacing aura while you're not incapacitated. The aura extends 10 feet from you in every direction, but not through Cover. If a creature is frightened of you, its speed is reduced to 0 while in the aura, and that creature takes psychic damage equal to half your paladin level if it starts its turn there.

features.name: Scornful Rebuke
features.description: Those who dare to strike you are psychically punished for their audacity. Whenever a creature hits you with an attack, that creature takes psychic damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1) if you're not incapacitated.

features.name: Invincible Conqueror
features.description: You receive the ability to harness extraordinary martial prowess. As an action, you may magically become an avatar of conquest, gaining the following benefits for 1 minute: You have resistance to all damage. When you take the Attack action on your turn, you may make one additional attack as part of that action.

subclasses.name: Oath of Redemption
subclasses.description: A warrior that uses words instead of the sword

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: Grants the Channel Divinity options listed for this oath or domain.

features.name: Emissary of Peace
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to augment your presence with divine power. As a bonus action, you grant yourself a +5 bonus to Charisma (Persuasion) checks for the next 10 minutes.

features.name: Oath Spells
features.description: Always prepares oath spells at the listed paladin levels: 3rd: sanctuary, sleep; 5th: calm emotions, hold person; 9th: counterspell, hypnotic pattern; 13th: Otiluke's resilient sphere, stoneskin; 17th: hold monster, wall of force.

features.name: Rebuke the Violent
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to rebuke those who use violence. Immediately after an attacker within 30 feet of you deals damage with an attack against a creature other than you, you may use your reaction to force the attacker to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the attacker takes radiant damage equal to the damage it just dealt.

features.name: Tenets of Redemption
features.description: Defines the oath's ideals and obligations; it does not add a separate combat mechanic.

features.name: Aura of the Guardian
features.description: When a creature within 10 feet of you takes damage, you may use your reaction to magically take that damage, instead of that creature taking it. This feature doesn't transfer any other effects that might accompany the damage, and this damage can't be reduced in any way. The range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

features.name: Protective Spirit
features.description: You regain hit points equal to 1d6 + half your paladin level if you end your turn in combat with fewer than half of your hit points remaining and you aren't incapacitated.

features.name: Emissary of Redemption
features.description: You become an avatar of peace, which gives you two benefits: You have resistance to all damage dealt by other creatures (their attacks, spells, and other effects). Whenever a creature hits you with an attack, it takes radiant damage equal to half the damage you take from the attack.

subclasses.name: Oath of Glory
subclasses.description: A warrior that is destined for glory, fight for your destiny

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: Grants the Channel Divinity options listed for this oath or domain.

features.name: Inspiring Smite
features.description: Immediately after you deal damage to a creature with your Divine Smite feature, you may use your Channel Divinity as a bonus action and distribute temporary hit points to creatures of your choice within 30 feet of you, which can include you. The total number of temporary hit points equals 2d8 + your level in this class, divided among the chosen creatures however you like.

features.name: Oath Spells
features.description: Always prepares oath spells at the listed paladin levels: 3rd: guiding bolt, heroism; 5th: enhance ability, magic weapon; 9th: haste, protection from energy; 13th: compulsion, freedom of movement; 17th: commune, flame strike.

features.name: Peerless Athlete
features.description: As a bonus action, you may use your Channel Divinity to augment your athleticism. For the next 10 minutes, you have advantage on Strength (Athletics) and Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks; you may carry, push, drag, and lift twice as much weight as normal; and the distance of your long and high jumps increases by 10 feet (this extra distance costs movement as normal).

features.name: Tenets of Glory
features.description: Defines the oath's ideals and obligations; it does not add a separate combat mechanic.

features.name: Aura of Alacrity
features.description: You emanate an aura that fills you and your companions with supernatural speed, allowing you to race across a battlefield in formation. Your walking speed increases by 10 feet. Also, if you aren't incapacitated, the walking speed of any ally who starts their turn within 5 feet of you increases by 10 feet until the end of that turn.

features.name: Glorious Defense
features.description: You may turn defense into a sudden strike. When you or another creature you may see within 10 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you may use your reaction to grant a bonus to the target's AC against that attack, potentially causing it to miss. The bonus equals your Charisma modifier (minimum of +1).

features.name: Living Legend
features.description: As a bonus action, you receive the following benefits for 1 minute: You are blessed with an otherworldly presence, gaining advantage on all Charisma checks. Once on each of your turns when you make a weapon attack and miss, you may cause that attack to hit instead. If you fail a saving throw, you may use your reaction to reroll it.

subclasses.name: Oath of the Watchers
subclasses.description: A warrior sworn to fight anything supernatural.

features.name: Abjure the Extraplanar
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to castigate unworldly beings. As an action, you present your holy symbol and each aberration, celestial, elemental, fey, or fiend within 30 feet of you that can hear you makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is turned for 1 minute or until it takes damage.

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: Grants the Channel Divinity options listed for this oath or domain.

features.name: Oath Spells
features.description: Always prepares oath spells at the listed paladin levels: 3rd: alarm, detect magic; 5th: moonbeam, see invisibility; 9th: counterspell, nondetection; 13th: aura of purity, banishment; 17th: hold monster, scrying.

features.name: Tenets of the Watchers
features.description: Defines the oath's ideals and obligations; it does not add a separate combat mechanic.

features.name: Watcher's Will
features.description: You may use your Channel Divinity to invest your presence with the warding power of your faith. As an action, you may choose a number of creatures you may see within 30 feet of you, up to a number equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). For 1 minute, you and the chosen creatures have advantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws.

features.name: Aura of the Sentinel
features.description: You emit an aura of alertness while you aren't incapacitated. When you and any creatures of your choice within 10 feet of you roll initiative, you all gain a bonus to initiative equal to your proficiency bonus. The range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

features.name: Vigilant Rebuke
features.description: You've learned how to chastise anyone who dares wield beguilements against you and your wards. Whenever you or a creature you may see within 30 feet of you succeeds on an Intelligence, a Wisdom, or a Charisma saving throw, you may use your reaction to deal 2d8 + your Charisma modifier force damage to the creature that forced the saving throw.

features.name: Mortal Bulwark
features.description: As a bonus action, you receive the following benefits for 1 minute: You receive truesight with a range of 120 feet. You have advantage on attack rolls against aberrations, celestials, elementals, fey, and fiends. When you hit a creature with an attack roll and deal damage to it, you may also force it to make a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC.

subclasses.name: Oath of the Crown
subclasses.description: A warrior that serves a crown or kingdom

features.name: Champion Challenge
features.description: As a bonus action, you issue a challenge that compels other creatures to do battle with you. Each creature of your choice that you may see within 30 feet of you makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a creature can't willingly move more than 30 feet away from you.

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: Grants the Channel Divinity options listed for this oath or domain.

features.name: Oath Spells
features.description: Always prepares oath spells at the listed paladin levels: 3rd: command, compelled duel; 5th: warding bond, zone of truth; 9th: aura of vitality, spirit guardians; 13th: banishment, guardian of faith; 17th: circle of power, geas.

features.name: Tenets of the Crown
features.description: Defines the oath's ideals and obligations; it does not add a separate combat mechanic.

features.name: Turn the Tide
features.description: As a bonus action, you may bolster injured creatures with your Channel Divinity. Each creature of your choice that can hear you within 30 feet of you regains hit points equal to 1d6 + your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1) if it has no more than half of its hit points.

features.name: Divine Allegiance
features.description: When a creature within 5 feet of you takes damage, you may use your reaction to magically substitute your own health for that of the target creature, causing that creature not to take the damage. Instead, you take the damage. This damage to you can't be reduced or prevented in any way.

features.name: Unyielding Spirit
features.description: You have advantage on saving throws to avoid becoming paralyzed or stunned.

features.name: Exalted Champion
features.description: Your presence on the field of battle is an inspiration to those dedicated to your cause. You may use your action to gain the following benefits for 1 hour: You have resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical weapons. Your allies have advantage on death saving throws while within 30 feet of you.

name: Ranger
description: Rangers are those that wander and live within the wild. Their skill to survive in harsh nature places is bested by none. Many rangers take on a role, usually in various ways to hunt special types of monsters.

subclasses.name: Fey Wanderer
subclasses.description: With the power of fey on their side, these rangers charm their foes

features.name: Dreadful Strikes
features.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon, you may deal an extra 1d4 psychic damage to the target, which can take this extra damage only once per turn. The extra damage increases to 1d6 when you reach 11th level in this class.

features.name: Fey Wanderer Magic
features.description: Adds subclass spells at the listed levels: 3rd: charm person; 5th: misty step; 9th: dispel magic; 13th: dimension door; 17th: mislead.

features.name: Otherworldly Glamour
features.description: As a result, whenever you make a Charisma check, you receive a bonus to the check equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1). Also, you receive proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Deception, Performance, or Persuasion.

features.name: Beguiling Twist
features.description: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened. Also, whenever you or a creature you may see within 120 feet of you succeeds on a saving throw against being charmed or frightened, you may use your reaction to force a different creature you may see within 120 feet of you to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC.

features.name: Fey Reinforcements
features.description: It doesn't count against the number of ranger spells you know, and you may cast it without a material component. You may also cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. Whenever you start casting the spell, you may modify it so that it doesn't require concentration.

features.name: Misty Wanderer
features.description: You may slip in and out of the Feywild to move in a blink of an eye: you may cast misty step without expending a spell slot. You may do so a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. Also, whenever you cast misty step, you may bring along one willing creature you may see within 5 feet of you.

subclasses.name: Swarmkeeper
subclasses.description: Insects, pixies or a similar swarm creature. These rangers take care of them

features.name: Gathered Swarm
features.description: While you're alive, the swarm remains in your space, crawling on you or flying and skittering around you within your space. You determine its appearance, or you generate its appearance by rolling on the Swarm Appearance table.

features.name: Swarmkeeper Magic
features.description: Adds subclass spells at the listed levels: 3rd: faerie fire; 5th: web; 9th: gaseous form; 13th: arcane eye; 17th: insect plague.

features.name: Writhing Tide
features.description: You may condense part of your swarm into a focused mass that lifts you up. As a bonus action, you receive a flying speed of 10 feet and can hover. This effect lasts for 1 minute or until you are incapacitated.

features.name: Mighty Swarm
features.description: Your Gathered Swarm grows mightier in the following ways: The damage of Gathered Swarm increases to 1d8. If a creature fails its saving throw against being moved by Gathered Swarm, you may also cause the swarm to knock the creature prone. When you are moved by Gathered Swarm, it gives you Cover until the start of your next turn.

features.name: Swarming Dispersal
features.description: When you take damage, you may use your reaction to give yourself resistance to that damage. You vanish into your swarm and then teleport to an unoccupied space that you may see within 30 feet of you, where you reappear with the swarm. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclasses.name: Gloom Stalker
subclasses.description: These hunters wander in the shadows to defeat creatures of the night

features.name: Dread Ambusher
features.description: You may give yourself a bonus to your initiative rolls equal to your Wisdom modifier. At the start of your first turn of each combat, your walking speed increases by 10 feet, which lasts until the end of that turn. If you take the Attack action on that turn, you may make one additional weapon attack as part of that action.

features.name: Gloom Stalker Magic
features.description: Adds subclass spells at the listed levels: 3rd: disguise self; 5th: rope trick; 9th: fear; 13th: greater invisibility; 17th: seeming.

features.name: Umbral Sight
features.description: You receive darkvision out to a range of 60 feet. If you already have darkvision from your race, its range increases by 30 feet.

features.name: Iron Mind
features.description: By 7th level, you have honed your ability to resist the mind-altering powers of your prey. You receive proficiency in Wisdom saving throws. If you already have this proficiency, you instead gain proficiency in Intelligence or Charisma saving throws (your choice).

features.name: Stalker's Flurry
features.description: You learn to attack with such unexpected speed that you may turn a miss into another strike. Once on each of your turns when you miss with a weapon attack, you may make another weapon attack as part of the same action.

features.name: Shadowy Dodge
features.description: Whenever a creature makes an attack roll against you and doesn't have advantage on the roll, you may use your reaction to impose disadvantage on it. You must use this feature before you know the outcome of the attack roll.

subclasses.name: Horizon Walker
subclasses.description: These hunters seek out creatures from other worlds to end them

features.name: Detect Portal
features.description: You receive the ability to magically sense the presence of a planar portal. As an action, you detect the distance and direction to the closest planar portal within 1 mile of you. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Horizon Walker Magic
features.description: Adds subclass spells at the listed levels: 3rd: protection from evil and good; 5th: misty step; 9th: haste; 13th: banishment; 17th: teleportation circle.

features.name: Planar Warrior
features.description: You learn to draw on the energy of the multiverse to augment your attacks. As a bonus action, choose one creature you may see within 30 feet of you. The next time you hit that creature on this turn with a weapon attack, all damage dealt by the attack becomes force damage, and the creature takes an extra 1d8 force damage from the attack.

features.name: Ethereal Step
features.description: You learn to step through the Ethereal Plane. As a bonus action, you may cast the etherealness spell with this feature, without expending a spell slot, but the spell ends at the end of the current turn. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Distant Strike
features.description: You receive the ability to pass between the planes in the blink of an eye. When you take the Attack action, you may teleport up to 10 feet before each attack to an unoccupied space you may see. If you attack at least two different creatures with the action, you may make one additional attack with it against a third creature.

features.name: Spectral Defense
features.description: Your ability to move between planes enables you to slip through the planar boundaries to lessen the harm done to you during battle. When you take damage from an attack, you may use your reaction to give yourself resistance to all of that attack's damage on this turn.

subclasses.name: Monster Slayer
subclasses.description: These warriors hunt down to slay powerful monsters

features.name: Hunter's Sense
features.description: You receive the ability to peer at a creature and magically discern how best to hurt it. As an action, choose one creature you may see within 60 feet of you. You immediately learn whether the creature has any damage immunities, resistances, or vulnerabilities and what they are.

features.name: Monster Slayer Magic
features.description: Adds subclass spells at the listed levels: 3rd: protection from evil and good; 5th: zone of truth; 9th: magic circle; 13th: banishment; 17th: hold monster.

features.name: Slayer's Prey
features.description: As a bonus action, you designate one creature you may see within 60 feet of you as the target of this feature. The first time each turn that you hit that target with a weapon attack, it takes an extra 1d6 damage from the weapon. This benefit lasts until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Supernatural Defense
features.description: You receive extra resilience against your prey's assaults on your mind and body. Whenever the target of your Slayer's Prey forces you to make a saving throw and whenever you make an ability check to escape that target's grapple, add 1d6 to your roll.

features.name: Magic-User's Nemesis
features.description: You receive the ability to thwart someone else's magic. When you see a creature casting a spell or teleporting within 60 feet of you, you may use your reaction to try to magically foil it. The creature must pass a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC, or its spell or teleport fails and is wasted.

features.name: Slayer's Counter
features.description: You receive the ability to counterattack when your prey tries to sabotage you. If the target of your Slayer's Prey forces you to make a saving throw, you may use your reaction to make one weapon attack against the quarry. You make this attack immediately before making the saving throw.

subclasses.name: Hunter
subclasses.description: A warrior who hones different parts of their fighting style to hunt

features.name: Colossus Slayer
features.description: Your tenacity can wear down the most potent foes. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, the creature takes an extra 1d8 damage if it's below its hit point maximum. You may deal this extra damage only once per turn.

features.name: Giant Killer
features.description: When a Large or larger creature within 5 feet of you hits or misses you with an attack, you may use your reaction to attack that creature immediately after its attack, provided that you may see the creature.

features.name: Horde Breaker
features.description: Once on each of your turns when you make a weapon attack, you may make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the original target and within range of your weapon.

features.name: Hunter's Prey
features.description: You choose one of this Hunter feature's listed options at this level.

features.name: Defensive Tactics
features.description: You choose one of this Hunter feature's listed options at this level.

features.name: Escape the Horde
features.description: Opportunity attacks against you are made with disadvantage.

features.name: Multiattack Defense
features.description: When a creature hits you with an attack, you receive a +4 bonus to AC against all subsequent attacks made by that creature for the rest of the turn.

features.name: Steel Will
features.description: You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.

features.name: Multiattack
features.description: You choose one of this Hunter feature's listed options at this level.

features.name: Volley
features.description: You may use your action to make a ranged attack against any number of creatures within 10 feet of a point you may see within your weapon's range. You must have ammunition for each target, as normal, and you make a separate attack roll for each target.

features.name: Whirlwind Attack
features.description: You may use your action to make a melee attack against any number of creatures within 5 feet of you, with a separate attack roll for each target.

features.name: Evasion
features.description: You may nimbly dodge out of the way of certain area effects, such as a red dragon's fiery breath or a lightning bolt spell. When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if you fail.

features.name: Stand Against the Tide
features.description: When a hostile creature misses you with a melee attack, you may use your reaction to force that creature to repeat the same attack against another creature (other than itself) of your choice.

features.name: Superior Hunter's Defense
features.description: You choose one of this Hunter feature's listed options at this level.

features.name: Uncanny Dodge
features.description: When an attacker that you may see hits you with an attack, you may use your reaction to halve the attack's damage against you.

subclasses.name: Beast Master
subclasses.description: A warrior who has gained the assistance of a spiritual beast.

features.name: Ranger's Companion
features.description: You receive a beast companion that accompanies you on your adventures and is trained to fight alongside you. Add your proficiency bonus to the beast's AC, attack rolls, and damage rolls, as well as to any saving throws and skills it is proficient in. Its hit point maximum equals its normal maximum or four times your ranger level, whichever is higher.

features.name: Exceptional Training
features.description: On any of your turns when your beast companion doesn't attack, you may use a bonus action to command the beast to take the Dash, Disengage, or Help action on its turn. Also, the beast's attacks now count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

features.name: Bestial Fury
features.description: When you command your beast companion to take the Attack action, the beast can make two attacks, or it can take the Multiattack action if it has that action.

features.name: Share Spells
features.description: When you cast a spell targeting yourself, you may also affect your beast companion with the spell if the beast is within 30 feet of you.

subclasses.name: Drakewarden
subclasses.description: A warrior who has gained the assistance of a powerful drake that follows them

features.name: Draconic Gift
features.description: The bond you share with your drake creates a connection to dragonkind, granting you understanding and empowering your presence. You receive the following benefits: Thaumaturgy: You learn the thaumaturgy cantrip, which is a ranger spell for you. Tongue of Dragons: You learn to speak, read, and write Draconic or one other language of your choice.

features.name: Drake Companion
features.description: As an action, you may magically summon the drake that is bound to you. It appears in an unoccupied space of your choice within 30 feet of you. See its game statistics in the accompanying Drake Companion stat block, which uses your proficiency bonus (PB) in several places.

features.name: Bond of Fang and Scale
features.description: The bond you share with your drake intensifies, protecting you and stoking the drake's fury. When you summon your drake, it grows wings on its back and gains a flying speed equal to its walking speed. Reflecting your special bond, you may use the drake as a mount if your size is Medium or smaller.

features.name: Drake's Breath
features.description: As an action, you may exhale a 30-foot cone of damaging breath or cause your drake to exhale it. Choose acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison damage (your choice doesn't have to match your drake's Draconic Essence). Each creature in the cone makes a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC, taking 8d6 damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

features.name: Perfected Bond
features.description: Your bond to your drake reaches the pinnacle of its power. While your drake is summoned, you and the drake gain the following benefits: Empowered Bite The drake's Bite attack deals an extra 1d6 damage of the type chosen for its Draconic Essence (for a total of 2d6 extra damage).

name: Rogue
description: Rogues are talented individuals who hone skills of the unethical or criminal. A rogue's skill in thieving, killing, etc. is rivaled by none as they become experts in their field.

subclasses.name: Thief
subclasses.description: Thieves are rogues who are experts at breaking into things and stealing

features.name: Fast Hands
features.description: You may use the bonus action granted by your Cunning Action to make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check, use your thieves' tools to disarm a trap or open a lock, or take the Use an Object action.

features.name: Second-Story Work
