features.description: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you receive the ability to climb faster than normal; climbing no longer costs you extra movement. Also, when you make a running jump, the distance you cover increases by a number of feet equal to your Dexterity modifier.

features.name: Supreme Sneak
features.description: You have advantage on a Dexterity (Stealth) check if you move no more than half your speed on the same turn.

features.name: Use Magic Device
features.description: By 13th level, you have learned enough about the workings of magic that you may improvise the use of items even when they are not intended for you. You ignore all class, race, and level requirements on the use of magic items.

features.name: Thief's Reflexes
features.description: When you reach 17th level, you have become adept at laying ambushes and quickly escaping danger. You may take two turns during the first round of any combat. You take your first turn at your normal initiative and your second turn at your initiative minus 10.

subclasses.name: Assassin
subclasses.description: Assassins are masters at killing while unseen

features.name: Assassinate
features.description: You have advantage on attack rolls against any creature that hasn't taken a turn in the combat yet. Also, any hit you score against a creature that is surprised is a critical hit.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you receive proficiency with the disguise kit and the poisoner's kit.

features.name: Infiltration Expertise
features.description: For example, you might acquire appropriate clothing, letters of introduction, and official-looking certification to establish yourself as a member of a trading house from a remote city so you may insinuate yourself into the company of other wealthy merchants.

features.name: Impostor
features.description: You receive the ability to unerringly mimic another person's speech, writing, and behavior. Your ruse is indiscernible to the casual observer. If a wary creature suspects something is amiss, you have advantage on any Charisma (Deception) check you make to avoid detection.

features.name: Death Strike
features.description: When you attack and hit a creature that is surprised, it makes a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 + your Dexterity modifier + your proficiency bonus). On a failed save, double the damage of your attack against the creature.

subclasses.name: Arcane Trickster
subclasses.description: Tricksters mix magic into their skills, enhancing them

features.name: Mage Hand Legerdemain
features.description: When you cast mage hand, you may make the spectral hand invisible, and you may perform the following additional tasks with it: You may stow one object the hand is holding in a container worn or carried by another creature. You may use thieves' tools to pick locks and disarm traps at range.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: Grants limited wizard spellcasting using the subclass progression, including cantrips and leveled spells with the listed school restrictions.

features.name: Magical Ambush
features.description: If you are hidden from a creature when you cast a spell on it, the creature has disadvantage on any saving throw it makes against the spell this turn.

features.name: Versatile Trickster
features.description: You receive the ability to distract targets with your mage hand. As a bonus action on your turn, you may designate a creature within 5 feet of the spectral hand created by the spell. Doing so gives you advantage on attack rolls against that creature until the end of the turn.

features.name: Spell Thief
features.description: You receive the ability to magically steal the knowledge of how to cast a spell from another spellcaster. Immediately after a creature casts a spell that targets you or includes you in its area of effect, you may use your reaction to force the creature to make a saving throw with its spellcasting ability modifier. The DC equals your spell save DC.

subclasses.name: Inquisitive
subclasses.description: Like a detective, inquisitive rogues watch their targets and use their patterns

features.name: Ear for Deceit
features.description: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you develop a talent for picking out lies. Whenever you make a Wisdom (Insight) check to determine whether a creature is lying, treat a roll of 7 or lower on the d20 as an 8.

features.name: Eye for Detail
features.description: You may use a bonus action to make a Wisdom (Perception) check to spot a hidden creature or object or to make an Intelligence (Investigation) check to uncover or decipher clues.

features.name: Insightful Fighting
features.description: You receive the ability to decipher an opponent's tactics and develop a counter to them. As a bonus action, you may make a Wisdom (Insight) check against a creature you may see that isn't incapacitated, contested by the target's Charisma (Deception) check. If you succeed, you may use your Sneak Attack against that target even if you don't have advantage on the attack roll, but not if you have disadvantage on it.

features.name: Steady Eye
features.description: You have advantage on any Wisdom (Perception) or Intelligence (Investigation) check if you move no more than half your speed on the same turn.

features.name: Unerring Eye
features.description: As an action, you sense the presence of illusions, shapechangers not in their original form, and other magic designed to deceive the senses within 30 feet of you, provided you aren't blinded or deafened. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Eye for Weakness
features.description: You learn to exploit a creature's weaknesses by carefully studying its tactics and movement. While your Insightful Fighting feature applies to a creature, your Sneak Attack damage against that creature increases by 3d6.

subclasses.name: Mastermind
subclasses.description: These rogues are deadly smart, using their intelligence to fight

features.name: Master of Intrigue
features.description: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you receive proficiency with the disguise kit, the forgery kit, and one gaming set of your choice. You also learn two languages of your choice. Also, you may unerringly mimic the speech patterns and accent of a creature that you hear speak for at least 1 minute, enabling you to pass yourself off as a native speaker of a particular land, provided that you know the language.

features.name: Master of Tactics
features.description: You may use the Help action as a bonus action. Also, when you use the Help action to aid an ally in attacking a creature, the target of that attack can be within 30 feet of you, rather than within 5 feet of you, if the target can see or hear you.

features.name: Insightful Manipulator
features.description: If you spend at least 1 minute observing or interacting with another creature outside combat, you may learn certain information about its capabilities compared to your own.

features.name: Misdirection
features.description: You may sometimes cause another creature to suffer an attack meant for you. When you are targeted by an attack while a creature within 5 feet of you is granting you cover against that attack, you may use your reaction to have the attack target that creature instead of you.

features.name: Soul of Deceit
features.description: You may present false thoughts by succeeding on a Charisma (Deception) check contested by the mind reader's Wisdom (Insight) check. Also, no matter what you say, magic that would determine if you are telling the truth indicates you are being truthful if you so choose, and you can't be compelled to tell the truth by magic.

subclasses.name: Scout
subclasses.description: Ambushing and Stealthing with speed, these rogues are always ahead

features.name: Skirmisher
features.description: You may move up to half your speed as a reaction when an enemy ends its turn within 5 feet of you. This movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.

features.name: Survivalist
features.description: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you receive proficiency in the Nature and Survival skills if you don't already have it. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of those proficiencies.

features.name: Superior Mobility
features.description: Your walking speed increases by 10 feet.

features.name: Ambush Master
features.description: You excel at leading ambushes and acting first in a fight. You have advantage on initiative rolls. Also, the first creature you hit during the first round of a combat becomes easier for you and others to strike; attack rolls against that target have advantage until the start of your next turn.

features.name: Sudden Strike
features.description: If you take the Attack action on your turn, you may make one additional attack as a bonus action. This attack can benefit from your Sneak Attack even if you have already used it this turn, but you can't use your Sneak Attack against the same target more than once in a turn.

subclasses.name: Swashbuckler
subclasses.description: Rogues that are fast and light on their feet, impossible to pin

features.name: Fancy Footwork
features.description: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you learn how to land a strike and then slip away without reprisal. During your turn, if you make a melee attack against a creature, that creature can't make opportunity attacks against you for the rest of your turn.

features.name: Rakish Audacity
features.description: You may give yourself a bonus to your initiative rolls equal to your Charisma modifier. You also gain an additional way to use your Sneak Attack; you don't need advantage on your attack roll to use Sneak Attack against a creature if you are within 5 feet of it, no other creatures are within 5 feet of you, and you don't have disadvantage on the attack roll. All the other rules for Sneak Attack still apply to you.

features.name: Panache
features.description: Your charm becomes extraordinarily beguiling. As an action, you may make a Charisma (Persuasion) check contested by a creature's Wisdom (Insight) check. If you succeed on the check and the creature is hostile to you, it has disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you and can't make opportunity attacks against targets other than you.

features.name: Elegant Maneuver
features.description: You may use a bonus action on your turn to gain advantage on the next Dexterity (Acrobatics) or Strength (Athletics) check you make during the same turn.

features.name: Master Duelist
features.description: Your mastery of the blade lets you turn failure into success in combat. If you miss with an attack roll, you may roll it again with advantage. Once you do so, you can't use this feature again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclasses.name: Phantom
subclasses.description: Shadow magic is within the hearts of these rogues, stealing souls.

features.name: Wails from the Grave
features.description: Immediately after you deal your Sneak Attack damage to a creature on your turn, you may target a second creature that you may see within 30 feet of the first creature. Roll half the number of Sneak Attack dice for your level (round up), and the second creature takes necrotic damage equal to the roll's total, as wails of the dead sound around them for a moment.

features.name: Whispers of the Dead
features.description: Echoes of those who have died cling to you. Whenever you finish a short or long rest, you may choose one skill or tool proficiency that you lack and gain it, as a ghostly presence shares its knowledge with you. You lose this proficiency when you use this feature to choose a different proficiency that you lack.

features.name: Tokens of the Departed
features.description: When a creature dies within 30 feet, you can use your reaction to create a soul trinket, up to your proficiency bonus at once. Soul trinkets can aid your death and Constitution saves, fuel Wails from the Grave without spending a use, or let you ask the spirit one question.

features.name: Ghost Walk
features.description: As a bonus action, you assume a spectral form. While in this form, you have a flying speed of 10 feet, you may hover, and attack rolls have disadvantage against you. You may also move through creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain, but you take 1d10 force damage if you end your turn inside a creature or an object.

features.name: Death's Friend
features.description: Your association with death has become so close that you receive the following benefits: When you use your Wails from the Grave, you may deal the necrotic damage to both the first and the second creature. At the end of a long rest, a soul trinket appears in your hand if you don't have any soul trinkets, as the spirits of the dead are drawn to you.

subclasses.name: Soulknife
subclasses.description: Blessed with a psychic mind, their roguish skills are enhanced

features.name: Psi-Bolstered Knack
features.description: When your non-psionic training fails you, your psionic power can help: if you fail an ability check using a skill or tool with which you have proficiency, you may roll one Psionic Energy die and add the number rolled to the check, potentially turning failure into success. You expend the die only if the roll succeeds.

features.name: Psionic Power
features.description: This energy is represented by your Psionic Energy dice, which are each a d6. You have a number of these dice equal to twice your proficiency bonus, and they fuel various psionic powers you have, which are detailed below. Some of your powers expend the Psionic Energy die they use, as specified in a power's description, and you can't use a power if it requires you to use a die when your dice are all expended.

features.name: Psychic Blades
features.description: Whenever you take the Attack action, you may manifest a psychic blade from your free hand and make the attack with that blade. It has a normal range of 60 feet and no long range, and on a hit, it deals psychic damage equal to 1d6 plus the ability modifier you used for the attack roll. The blade vanishes immediately after it hits or misses its target, and it leaves no mark on its target if it deals damage.

features.name: Psychic Whispers
features.description: As an action, choose one or more creatures you may see, up to a number of creatures equal to your proficiency bonus, and then roll one Psionic Energy die. For a number of hours equal to the number rolled, the chosen creatures can speak telepathically with you, and you may speak telepathically with them. To send or receive a message (no action required), you and the other creature must be within 1 mile of each other.

features.name: Homing Strikes
features.description: If you make an attack roll with your Psychic Blades and miss the target, you may roll one Psionic Energy die and add the number rolled to the attack roll. If this causes the attack to hit, you expend the Psionic Energy die.

features.name: Psychic Teleportation
features.description: As a bonus action, you manifest one of your Psychic Blades, expend one Psionic Energy die and roll it, and throw the blade at an unoccupied space you may see, up to a number of feet away equal to 10 times the number rolled. You then teleport to that space, and the blade vanishes.

features.name: Soul Blades
features.description: Your Psychic Blades gain Homing Strikes and Psychic Teleportation, both powered by your Psionic Energy dice.

features.name: Psychic Veil
features.description: As an action, you may magically become invisible, along with anything you are wearing or carrying, for 1 hour or until you dismiss this effect (no action required). This invisibility ends early immediately after you deal damage to a creature or you force a creature to make a saving throw.

features.name: Rend Mind
features.description: When you use your Psychic Blades to deal Sneak Attack damage to a creature, you may force that target to make a Wisdom saving throw (DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Dexterity modifier). If the save fails, the target is stunned for 1 minute. The stunned target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

name: Sorcerer
description: Usually born with magical energy running through their veins. Sorcerers are what people usually think of when the image of a "spellslinger" comes to mind. Their magical force is linked to their emotions and mind.

subclasses.name: Aberrant Mind
subclasses.description: Cursed or blessed with a powerful psychic mind, their magic comes from their head

features.name: Psionic Spells
features.description: Adds subclass spells at the listed levels: 1st: arms of Hadar, dissonant whispers, mind sliver; 3rd: calm emotions, detect thoughts; 5th: hunger of Hadar, sending; 7th: Evard's black tentacles, summon aberration; 9th: Rary's telepathic bond, telekinesis.

features.name: Telepathic Speech
features.description: As a bonus action, choose one creature you may see within 30 feet of you. You and the chosen creature can speak telepathically with each other while the two of you are within a number of miles of each other equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1 mile). To understand each other, you each must speak mentally in a language the other knows.

features.name: Psionic Sorcery
features.description: When you cast any spell of 1st level or higher from your Psionic Spells feature, you may cast it by expending a spell slot as normal or by spending a number of sorcery points equal to the spell's level. If you cast the spell using sorcery points, it requires no verbal or somatic components, and it requires no material components, unless they are consumed by the spell.

features.name: Psychic Defenses
features.description: You receive resistance to psychic damage, and you have advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened.

features.name: Revelation in Flesh
features.description: As a bonus action, you may spend 1 or more sorcery points to magically transform your body for 10 minutes. For each sorcery point you spend, you may gain one of the following benefits of your choice, the effects of which last until the transformation ends: You may see any invisible creature within 60 feet of you, provided it isn't behind Cover. Your eyes also turn black or become writhing sensory tendrils.

features.name: Warping Implosion
features.description: You may unleash your aberrant power as a space-warping anomaly. As an action, you may teleport to an unoccupied space you may see within 120 feet of you. Immediately after you disappear, each creature within 30 feet of the space you left makes a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC.

subclasses.name: Clockwork Soul
subclasses.description: The balance of the universe flows through their veins

features.name: Clockwork Magic
features.description: Adds subclass spells at the listed levels: 1st: alarm, protection from evil and good; 3rd: aid, lesser restoration; 5th: dispel magic, protection from energy; 7th: freedom of movement, summon construct; 9th: greater restoration, wall of force.

features.name: Restore Balance
features.description: When a creature you may see within 60 feet of you is about to roll a d20 with advantage or disadvantage, you may use your reaction to prevent the roll from being affected by advantage and disadvantage. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Bastion of Law
features.description: As an action, you may expend 1 to 5 sorcery points to create a magical ward around yourself or another creature you may see within 30 feet of you. The ward lasts until you finish a long rest or until you use this feature again. The ward is represented by a number of d8s equal to the number of sorcery points spent to create it.

features.name: Trance of Order
features.description: You receive the ability to align your consciousness to the endless calculations of Mechanus. As a bonus action, you may enter this state for 1 minute. For the duration, attack rolls against you can't benefit from advantage, and whenever you make an attack roll, an ability check, or a saving throw, you may treat a roll of 9 or lower on the d20 as a 10.

features.name: Clockwork Cavalcade
features.description: As an action, you summon the spirits in a 30-foot cube originating from you. The spirits are intangible and invulnerable, and they create the following effects within the cube before vanishing: The spirits restore up to 100 hit points, divided as you choose among any number of creatures of your choice in the cube. Any damaged objects entirely in the cube are repaired instantly.

subclasses.name: Divine Soul
subclasses.description: With blood from something Divine, gods watch them with interest

features.name: Divine Magic
features.description: Your link to the divine allows you to learn spells from the cleric class. When your Spellcasting feature lets you learn or replace a sorcerer cantrip or a sorcerer spell of 1st level or higher, you may choose the new spell from the cleric spell list or the sorcerer spell list. You must otherwise obey all the restrictions for selecting the spell, and it becomes a sorcerer spell for you.

features.name: Favored by the Gods
features.description: If you fail a saving throw or miss with an attack roll, you may roll 2d4 and add it to the total, possibly changing the outcome. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Empowered Healing
features.description: The divine energy coursing through you may empower healing spells. Whenever you or an ally within 5 feet of you rolls dice to determine the number of hit points a spell restores, you may spend 1 sorcery point to reroll any number of those dice once, provided you aren't incapacitated. You may use this feature only once per turn.

features.name: Otherworldly Wings
features.description: You may use a bonus action to manifest a pair of spectral wings from your back. While the wings are present, you have a flying speed of 30 feet. The wings last until you're incapacitated, you die, or you dismiss them as a bonus action.

features.name: Unearthly Recovery
features.description: You receive the ability to overcome grievous injuries. As a bonus action when you have fewer than half of your hit points remaining, you may regain a number of hit points equal to half your hit point maximum. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclasses.name: Shadow Magic
subclasses.description: Cursed with shadow magic, their power manipulates the shadows

features.name: Eyes of the Dark
features.description: You have darkvision with a range of 120 feet. When you reach 3rd level in this class, you learn the darkness spell, which doesn't count against your number of sorcerer spells known. Also, you may cast it by spending 2 sorcery points or by expending a spell slot.

features.name: Strength of the Grave
features.description: When damage reduces you to 0 hit points, you may make a Charisma saving throw (DC 5 + the damage taken). On a success, you instead drop to 1 hit point. You can't use this feature if you are reduced to 0 hit points by radiant damage or by a critical hit.

features.name: Hound of Ill Omen
features.description: You receive the ability to call forth a howling creature of darkness to harass your foes. As a bonus action, you may spend 3 sorcery points to magically summon a hound of ill omen to target one creature you may see within 120 feet of you.

features.name: Shadow Walk
features.description: You receive the ability to step from one shadow into another. When you are in dim light or darkness, as a bonus action, you may magically teleport up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space you may see that is also in dim light or darkness.

features.name: Umbral Form
features.description: You may spend 6 sorcery points as a bonus action to magically transform yourself into a shadowy form. In this form, you have resistance to all damage except force and radiant damage, and you may move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. You take 5 force damage if you end your turn inside an object.

subclasses.name: Storm Sorcery
subclasses.description: With the blood of a powerful hurricane, storms are a comfort

features.name: Tempestuous Magic
features.description: You may use a bonus action on your turn to cause whirling gusts of elemental air to briefly surround you, immediately before or after you cast a spell of 1st level or higher. Doing so allows you to fly up to 10 feet without provoking opportunity attacks.

features.name: Wind Speaker
features.description: You can speak, read, and write Primordial, including its Aquan, Auran, Ignan, and Terran dialects.

features.name: Heart of the Storm
features.description: You receive resistance to lightning and thunder damage. Also, whenever you start casting a spell of 1st level or higher that deals lightning or thunder damage, stormy magic erupts from you. This eruption causes creatures of your choice that you may see within 10 feet of you to take lightning or thunder damage (choose each time this ability activates) equal to half your sorcerer level.

features.name: Storm Guide
features.description: You receive the ability to subtly control the weather around you. If it is raining, you may use an action to cause the rain to stop falling in a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on you. You may end this effect as a bonus action.

features.name: Storm's Fury
features.description: When you are hit by a melee attack, you may use your reaction to deal lightning damage to the attacker. The damage equals your sorcerer level. The attacker must also make a Strength saving throw against your sorcerer spell save DC.

features.name: Wind Soul
features.description: You receive immunity to lightning and thunder damage. You also gain a magical flying speed of 60 feet. As an action, you may reduce your flying speed to 30 feet for 1 hour and choose a number of creatures within 30 feet of you equal to 3 + your Charisma modifier.

subclasses.name: Draconic Bloodline
subclasses.description: With the ancestors of dragons, they show draconic natures

features.name: Draconic Resilience
features.description: As magic flows through your body, it causes physical traits of your dragon ancestors to emerge. Your hit point maximum increases by 1 and increases by 1 again whenever you receive a level in this class. Also, parts of your skin are covered by a thin sheen of dragon-like scales.

features.name: Dragon Ancestor
features.description: The damage type associated with each dragon is used by features you receive later. Draconic Ancestry; Black: Acid; Blue: Lightning; Brass: Fire; Bronze: Lightning; Copper: Acid; Gold: Fire; Green: Poison; Red: Fire; Silver: Cold; White: Cold You may speak, read, and write Draconic. Also, whenever you make a Charisma check when interacting with dragons, your proficiency bonus is doubled if it applies to the check.

features.name: Elemental Affinity
features.description: When you cast a spell that deals damage of the type associated with your draconic ancestry, you may add your Charisma modifier to one damage roll of that spell. At the same time, you may spend 1 sorcery point to gain resistance to that damage type for 1 hour.

features.name: Dragon Wings
features.description: You receive the ability to sprout a pair of dragon wings from your back, gaining a flying speed equal to your current speed. You may create these wings as a bonus action on your turn. They last until you dismiss them as a bonus action on your turn.

features.name: Draconic Presence
features.description: You may channel the dread presence of your dragon ancestor, causing those around you to become awestruck or frightened. As an action, you may spend 5 sorcery points to draw on this power and exude an aura of awe or fear (your choice) to a distance of 60 feet.

subclasses.name: Wild Magic
subclasses.description: The chaos of the universe flows through their veins.

features.name: Tides of Chaos
features.description: You may manipulate the forces of chance and chaos to gain advantage on one attack roll, ability check, or saving throw. Once you do so, you must finish a long rest before you may use this feature again. Any time before you regain the use of this feature, the DM can have you roll on the Wild Magic Surge table immediately after you cast a sorcerer spell of 1st level or higher.

features.name: Wild Magic Surge
features.description: Starting when you choose this origin at 1st level, your spellcasting can unleash surges of untamed magic. Immediately after you cast a sorcerer spell of 1st level or higher, the DM can have you roll a d20. If you roll a 1, roll on the Wild Magic Surge table to create a random magical effect.

features.name: Bend Luck
features.description: You have the ability to twist fate using your wild magic. When another creature you may see makes an attack roll, an ability check, or a saving throw, you may use your reaction and spend 2 sorcery points to roll 1d4 and apply the number rolled as a bonus or penalty (your choice) to the creature's roll. You may do so after the creature rolls but before any effects of the roll occur.

features.name: Controlled Chaos
features.description: Whenever you roll on the Wild Magic Surge table, you may roll twice and use either number.

features.name: Spell Bombardment
features.description: The harmful energy of your spells intensifies. When you roll damage for a spell and roll the highest number possible on any of the dice, choose one of those dice, roll it again and add that roll to the damage. You may use the feature only once per turn.

name: Warlock
description: With the dark stereotype of being evil, warlocks are feared. Usually, these are mortals who have signed a magical contract with immortal entities, in which the entity will grant them magical power in exchange for servitude.

subclasses.name: The Archfey
subclasses.description: A magical pact with an Archfey of the feywild

features.name: Expanded Spell List
features.description: Adds patron spells to the warlock spell options at each spell level: 1st: faerie fire, sleep; 2nd: calm emotions, phantasmal force; 3rd: blink, plant growth; 4th: dominate beast, greater invisibility; 5th: dominate person, seeming.

features.name: Fey Presence
features.description: Your patron bestows upon you the ability to project the beguiling and fearsome presence of the fey. As an action, you may cause each creature in a 10-foot cube originating from you to make a Wisdom saving throw against your warlock spell save DC. The creatures that fail their saving throws are all charmed or frightened by you (your choice) until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Misty Escape
features.description: When you take damage, you may use your reaction to turn invisible and teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you may see. You remain invisible until the start of your next turn or until you attack or cast a spell. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Beguiling Defenses
features.description: Your patron teaches you how to turn the mind-affecting magic of your enemies against them. You are immune to being charmed, and when another creature attempts to charm you, you may use your reaction to attempt to turn the charm back on that creature. The creature must pass a Wisdom saving throw against your warlock spell save DC or be charmed by you for 1 minute or until the creature takes any damage.

features.name: Dark Delirium
features.description: As an action, choose a creature that you may see within 60 feet of you. It makes a Wisdom saving throw against your warlock spell save DC. On a failed save, it is charmed or frightened by you (your choice) for 1 minute or until your concentration is broken (as if you are concentrating on a spell).

subclasses.name: The Fiend
subclasses.description: A magical pact with a demonic or devilish entity

features.name: Dark One's Blessing
features.description: When you reduce a hostile creature to 0 hit points, you receive temporary hit points equal to your Charisma modifier + your warlock level (minimum of 1).

features.name: Expanded Spell List
features.description: Adds patron spells to the warlock spell options at each spell level: 1st: burning hands, command; 2nd: blindness/deafness, scorching ray; 3rd: fireball, stinking cloud; 4th: fire shield, wall of fire; 5th: flame strike, hallow.

features.name: Dark One's Own Luck
features.description: When you make an ability check or a saving throw, you may use this feature to add a d10 to your roll. You may do so after seeing the initial roll but before any of the roll's effects occur. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Fiendish Resilience
features.description: You may choose one damage type when you finish a short or long rest. You receive resistance to that damage type until you choose a different one with this feature. Damage from magical weapons or silver weapons ignores this resistance.

features.name: Hurl Through Hell
features.description: When you hit a creature with an attack, you may use this feature to instantly transport the target through the lower planes. At the end of your next turn, the target returns to the space it previously occupied, or the nearest unoccupied space. If the target is not a fiend, it takes 10d10 psychic damage as it reels from its horrific experience.

subclasses.name: The Great Old One
subclasses.description: An ancient and powerful unknown entity from worlds beyond

features.name: Awakened Mind
features.description: Your alien knowledge gives you the ability to touch the minds of other creatures. You may telepathically speak to any creature you may see within 30 feet of you.

features.name: Expanded Spell List
features.description: Adds patron spells to the warlock spell options at each spell level: 1st: dissonant whispers, Tasha's hideous laughter; 2nd: detect thoughts, phantasmal force; 3rd: clairvoyance, sending; 4th: dominate beast, Evard's black tentacles; 5th: dominate person, telekinesis.

features.name: Entropic Ward
features.description: You learn to magically ward yourself against attack and to turn an enemy's failed strike into good luck for yourself. When a creature makes an attack roll against you, you may use your reaction to impose disadvantage on that roll. If the attack misses you, your next attack roll against the creature has advantage if you make it before the end of your next turn.

features.name: Thought Shield
features.description: You also have resistance to psychic damage, and whenever a creature deals psychic damage to you, that creature takes the same amount of damage that you do.

features.name: Create Thrall
features.description: You receive the ability to infect a humanoid's mind with the alien magic of your patron. You may use your action to touch an incapacitated humanoid. That creature is then charmed by you until a remove curse spell is cast on it, the charmed condition is removed from it, or you use this feature again.

subclasses.name: The Celestial
subclasses.description: A magical pact with an entity of good, law, and order.

features.name: Bonus Cantrips
features.description: You learn the sacred flame and light cantrips.

features.name: Expanded Spell List
features.description: Adds patron spells to the warlock spell options at each spell level: 1st: cure wounds, guiding bolt; 2nd: flaming sphere, lesser restoration; 3rd: daylight, revivify; 4th: guardian of faith, wall of fire; 5th: flame strike, greater restoration.

features.name: Healing Light
features.description: You receive the ability to channel celestial energy to heal wounds. The number of dice in the pool equals 1 + your warlock level. As a bonus action, you may heal one creature you may see within 60 feet of you, spending dice from the pool.

features.name: Radiant Soul
features.description: You have resistance to radiant damage, and when you cast a spell that deals radiant or fire damage, you may add your Charisma modifier to one radiant or fire damage roll of that spell against one of its targets.

features.name: Celestial Resilience
features.description: You receive temporary hit points whenever you finish a short or long rest. These temporary hit points equal your warlock level + your Charisma modifier. Also, choose up to five creatures you may see at the end of the rest.

features.name: Searing Vengeance
features.description: When you have to make a death saving throw at the start of your turn, you may instead spring back to your feet with a burst of radiant energy. You regain hit points equal to half your hit point maximum, and then you stand up if you so choose. Each creature of your choice that is within 30 feet of you takes radiant damage equal to 2d8 + your Charisma modifier, and it is blinded until the end of the current turn.

subclasses.name: Undying
subclasses.description: A magical pact with an entity that represents the dead.

features.name: Among the Dead
features.description: You learn the spare the dying cantrip, which counts as a warlock cantrip for you. You also have advantage on saving throws against any disease. Also, undead have difficulty harming you.

features.name: Expanded Spell List
features.description: Adds patron spells to the warlock spell options at each spell level: 1st: false life, ray of sickness; 2nd: blindness/deafness, silence; 3rd: feign death, speak with dead; 4th: aura of life, death ward; 5th: contagion, legend lore.

features.name: Defy Death
features.description: You may regain hit points equal to 1d8 + your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1 hit point) when you succeed on a death saving throw or when you stabilize a creature with spare the dying. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Undying Nature
features.description: You may hold your breath indefinitely, and you don't require food, water, or sleep, although you still require rest to reduce exhaustion and still benefit from finishing short and long rests.

features.name: Indestructible Life
features.description: When you reach 14th level, you partake some of the true secrets of the Undying. On your turn, you may use a bonus action to regain hit points equal to 1d8 + your warlock level. Also, if you put a severed body part of yours back in place when you use this feature, the part reattaches.

subclasses.name: The Hexblade
subclasses.description: A magical pact with an entity that is or gifted a powerful weapon.

features.name: Expanded Spell List
features.description: Adds patron spells to the warlock spell options at each spell level: 1st: shield, wrathful smite; 2nd: blur, branding smite; 3rd: blink, elemental weapon; 4th: phantasmal killer, staggering smite; 5th: banishing smite, cone of cold.

features.name: Hex Warrior
features.description: You acquire the training necessary to effectively arm yourself for battle. You receive proficiency with medium armor, shields, and martial weapons. Whenever you finish a long rest, you may touch one weapon that you are proficient with and that lacks the two-handed property.

features.name: Hexblade's Curse
features.description: You receive the ability to place a baleful curse on someone. As a bonus action, choose one creature you may see within 30 feet of you. The target is cursed for 1 minute.

features.name: Accursed Specter
features.description: When you slay a humanoid, you may cause its spirit to rise from its corpse as a specter, the statistics for which are in the Monster Manual. When the specter appears, it gains temporary hit points equal to half your warlock level. Roll initiative for the specter, which has its own turns.

features.name: Armor of Hexes
features.description: If the target cursed by your Hexblade's Curse hits you with an attack roll, you may use your reaction to roll a d6. On a 4 or higher, the attack instead misses you, regardless of its roll.

features.name: Master of Hexes
features.description: When the creature cursed by your Hexblade's Curse dies, you may apply the curse to a different creature you may see within 30 feet of you, provided you aren't incapacitated. When you apply the curse in this way, you don't regain hit points from the death of the previously cursed creature.

subclasses.name: The Fathomless
subclasses.description: A magical pact with a creature of the world's deep oceans.

features.name: Expanded Spell List
features.description: Adds patron spells to the warlock spell options at each spell level: 1st: create or destroy water, thunderwave; 2nd: gust of wind, silence; 3rd: lightning bolt, sleet storm; 4th: control water, summon elemental; 5th: Bigby's hand, cone of cold.

features.name: Gift of the Sea
features.description: You receive a swimming speed of 40 feet, and you may breathe underwater.

features.name: Tentacle of the Deeps
features.description: You may magically summon a spectral tentacle that strikes at your foes. As a bonus action, you create a 10-foot-long tentacle at a point you may see within 60 feet of you. The tentacle lasts for 1 minute or until you use this feature to create another tentacle.

features.name: Guardian Coil
features.description: Your Tentacle of the Deeps can defend you and others, interposing itself between them and harm. When you or a creature you may see takes damage while within 10 feet of the tentacle, you may use your reaction to choose one of those creatures and reduce the damage to that creature by 1d8. When you reach 10th level in this class, the damage reduced by the tentacle increases to 2d8.

features.name: Oceanic Soul
features.description: You receive resistance to cold damage.

features.name: Grasping Tentacles
features.description: You learn the spell Evard's black tentacles. It counts as a warlock spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of spells you know. You may also cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Fathomless Plunge
features.description: You may magically open temporary conduits to watery destinations. As an action, you may teleport yourself and up to five other willing creatures that you may see within 30 feet of you. Amid a whirl of tentacles, you all vanish and then reappear up to 1 mile away in a body of water you've seen (pond size or larger) or within 30 feet of it, each of you appearing in an unoccupied space within 30 feet of the others.

subclasses.name: The Genie
subclasses.description: A magical pact with a powerful elemental.

features.name: Bottled Respite
features.description: As an action, you may magically vanish and enter your vessel, which remains in the space you left. The interior of the vessel is an extradimensional space in the shape of a 20-foot-radius cylinder, 20 feet high, and resembles your vessel. While inside, you may hear the area around your vessel as if you were in its space.

features.name: Expanded Spell List
features.description: Adds patron spells to the warlock spell options at each spell level: 1st: detect evil and good, sanctuary, thunderwave, burning hands, fog cloud; 2nd: phantasmal force, spike growth, gust of wind, scorching ray, blur; 3rd: create food and water, meld into stone, wind wall, fireball, sleet storm; 4th: phantasmal killer, stone shape, greater invisibility, fire shield, control water; 5th: creation, wall of stone, seeming, flame strike, cone of cold; 9th: wish.

features.name: Genie's Vessel
features.description: The vessel is a Tiny object, and you may use it as a spellcasting focus for your warlock spells. You decide what the object is, or you may determine what it is randomly by rolling on the Genie's Vessel table.

features.name: Genie's Wrath
features.description: Once during each of your turns when you hit with an attack roll, you may deal extra damage to the target equal to your proficiency bonus. The type of this damage is determined by your patron: bludgeoning (dao), thunder (djinni), fire (efreeti), or cold (marid).

features.name: Elemental Gift
features.description: You begin to take on characteristics of your patron's kind. You now have resistance to a damage type determined by your patron's kind: bludgeoning (dao), thunder (djinni), fire (efreeti), or cold (marid). Also, as a bonus action, you may give yourself a flying speed of 30 feet that lasts for 10 minutes, during which you may hover.

features.name: Sanctuary Vessel
features.description: , you may now choose up to five willing creatures that you may see within 30 feet of you, and the chosen creatures are drawn into the vessel with you. As a bonus action, you may eject any number of creatures from the vessel, and everyone is ejected if you leave or die or if the vessel is destroyed.

features.name: Limited Wish
features.description: As an action, you may speak your desire to your Genie's Vessel, requesting the effect of one spell that is 6th level or lower and has a casting time of 1 action. The spell can be from any class's spell list, and you don't need to meet the requirements in that spell, including costly components; the spell simply takes effect as part of this action.

subclasses.name: The Undead
subclasses.description: A magical pact with a powerful deathless being that defies the cycle

features.name: Expanded Spell List
features.description: Adds patron spells to the warlock spell options at each spell level: 1st: bane, false life; 2nd: blindness/deafness, phantasmal force; 3rd: phantom steed, speak with dead; 4th: death ward, greater invisibility; 5th: antilife shell, cloudkill.

features.name: Form of Dread
features.description: As a bonus action, you transform for 1 minute. You receive the following benefits while transformed: You receive temporary hit points equal to 1d10 + your warlock level. Once during each of your turns, when you hit a creature with an attack roll, you may force it to make a Wisdom saving throw, and if the saving throw fails, the target is frightened of you until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Grave Touched
features.description: Also, once during each of your turns, when you hit a creature with an attack roll and roll damage against the creature, you may replace the damage type with necrotic damage. While you are using your Form of Dread, you may roll one additional damage die when determining the necrotic damage the target takes.

features.name: Necrotic Husk
features.description: You have resistance to necrotic damage. If you are transformed using your Form of Dread, you instead become immune to necrotic damage. Also, when you would be reduced to 0 hit points, you may use your reaction to drop to 1 hit point instead and cause your body to erupt with deathly energy.

features.name: Spirit Projection
features.description: As an action, you may project your spirit from your body. Any damage or other effects that apply to your spirit or physical body affects the other. Your spirit can remain outside your body for up to 1 hour or until your concentration is broken (as if concentration on a spell).

name: Wizard
description: Wizards are considered the Apex of the Arcane and spend a lot of their life in education and schooling. With their massive knowledge of magic, they know exactly how to shape magical forces to do what they desire. Most of the time, wizards tend to walk down a school of magic to master in.

subclasses.name: School of Abjuration
subclasses.description: Study the defensive powers of magic

features.name: Abjuration Savant
features.description: Copying abjuration spells into your spellbook costs half the usual gold and time.

features.name: Arcane Ward
features.description: When you cast an abjuration spell of 1st level or higher, you may simultaneously use a strand of the spell's magic to create a magical ward on yourself that lasts until you finish a long rest. The ward has a hit point maximum equal to twice your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier. Whenever you take damage, the ward takes the damage instead.

features.name: Projected Ward
features.description: When a creature that you may see within 30 feet of you takes damage, you may use your reaction to cause your Arcane Ward to absorb that damage. If this damage reduces the ward to 0 hit points, the warded creature takes any remaining damage.

features.name: Improved Abjuration
features.description: When you cast an abjuration spell that requires you to make an ability check as a part of casting that spell (as in counterspell and dispel magic), you add your proficiency bonus to that ability check.

features.name: Spell Resistance
features.description: You have advantage on saving throws against spells. Furthermore, you have resistance against the damage of spells.

subclasses.name: School of Conjuration
subclasses.description: Study the ways to control the creatures you summon

features.name: Conjuration Savant
features.description: Copying conjuration spells into your spellbook costs half the usual gold and time.

features.name: Minor Conjuration
features.description: Starting at 2nd level when you select this school, you may use your action to conjure up an inanimate object in your hand or on the ground in an unoccupied space that you may see within 10 feet of you. This object can be no larger than 3 feet on a side and weigh no more than 10 pounds, and its form must be that of a nonmagical object that you have seen.

features.name: Benign Transposition
features.description: You may use your action to teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space that you may see. Alternatively, you may choose a space within range that is occupied by a Small or Medium creature. If that creature is willing, you both teleport, swapping places.

features.name: Focused Conjuration
features.description: While you are concentrating on a conjuration spell, your concentration can't be broken as a result of taking damage.

features.name: Durable Summons
features.description: Any creature that you summon or create with a conjuration spell has 30 temporary hit points.

subclasses.name: School of Divination
subclasses.description: Study the ways magic can show you the future and past

features.name: Divination Savant
features.description: Copying divination spells into your spellbook costs half the usual gold and time.

features.name: Portent
features.description: When you finish a long rest, roll two d20s and record the numbers rolled. You may replace any attack roll, saving throw, or ability check made by you or a creature that you may see with one of these foretelling rolls. You must choose to do so before the roll, and you may replace a roll in this way only once per turn.

features.name: Expert Divination
features.description: Casting divination spells comes so easily to you that it expends only a fraction of your spellcasting efforts. When you cast a divination spell of 2nd level or higher using a spell slot, you regain one expended spell slot. The slot you regain must be of a level lower than the spell you cast and can't be higher than 5th level.

features.name: The Third Eye
features.description: You may use your action to increase your powers of perception. When you do so, choose one of the following benefits, which lasts until you are incapacitated or you take a short or long rest. You can't use the feature again until you finish a rest.

features.name: Greater Portent
features.description: The visions in your dreams intensify and paint a more accurate picture in your mind of what is to come. You roll three d20s for your Portent feature, rather than two.

subclasses.name: School of Enchantment
subclasses.description: Study the ways that magic can bend the minds of others

features.name: Enchantment Savant
features.description: Copying enchantment spells into your spellbook costs half the usual gold and time.

features.name: Hypnotic Gaze
features.description: As an action, choose one creature that you may see within 5 feet of you. If the target can see or hear you, it must pass a Wisdom saving throw against your wizard spell save DC or be charmed by you until the end of your next turn. The charmed creature's speed drops to 0, and the creature is incapacitated and visibly dazed.

features.name: Instinctive Charm
features.description: When a creature you may see within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll against you, you may use your reaction to divert the attack, provided that another creature is within the attack's range. The attacker makes a Wisdom saving throw against your wizard spell save DC. On a failed save, the attacker must target the creature that is closest to it, not including you or itself.

features.name: Split Enchantment
features.description: When you cast an enchantment spell of 1st level or higher that targets only one creature, you may have it target a second creature.

features.name: Alter Memories
features.description: You receive the ability to make a creature unaware of your magical influence on it. When you cast an enchantment spell to charm one or more creatures, you may alter one creature's understanding so that it remains unaware of being charmed. Also, once before the spell expires, you may use your action to try to make the chosen creature forget some of the time it spent charmed.

subclasses.name: School of Evocation
subclasses.description: Study the ways that magic can destroy and offense

features.name: Evocation Savant
features.description: Copying evocation spells into your spellbook costs half the usual gold and time.

features.name: Sculpt Spells
features.description: You may create pockets of relative safety within the effects of your evocation spells. When you cast an evocation spell that affects other creatures that you may see, you may choose a number of them equal to 1 + the spell's level. The chosen creatures automatically succeed on their saving throws against the spell, and they take no damage if they would normally take half damage on a successful save.

features.name: Potent Cantrip
features.description: When a creature succeeds on a saving throw against your cantrip, the creature takes half the cantrip's damage (if any) but suffers no additional effect from the cantrip.

features.name: Empowered Evocation
features.description: You may add your Intelligence modifier to one damage roll of any wizard evocation spell you cast.

features.name: Overchannel
features.description: You may increase the power of your simpler spells. When you cast a wizard spell of 1st through 5th-level that deals damage, you may deal maximum damage with that spell. If you use this feature again before you finish a long rest, you take 2d12 necrotic damage for each level of the spell, immediately after you cast it.

subclasses.name: School of Illusion
subclasses.description: Study the ways that magic can make the eyes of others lie

features.name: Illusion Savant
features.description: Copying illusion spells into your spellbook costs half the usual gold and time.

features.name: Improved Minor Illusion
features.description: When you choose this school at 2nd level, you learn the minor illusion cantrip. If you already know this cantrip, you learn a different wizard cantrip of your choice. When you cast minor illusion, you may create both a sound and an image with a single casting of the spell.

features.name: Malleable Illusions
features.description: When you cast an illusion spell that has a duration of 1 minute or longer, you may use your action to change the nature of that illusion (using the spell's normal parameters for the illusion), provided that you may see the illusion.

features.name: Illusory Self
features.description: You may create an illusory duplicate of yourself as an instant, almost instinctual reaction to danger. When a creature makes an attack roll against you, you may use your reaction to interpose the illusory duplicate between the attacker and yourself. The attack automatically misses you, then the illusion dissipates.

features.name: Illusory Reality
features.description: By 14th level, you have learned the secret of weaving shadow magic into your illusions to give them a semi-reality. When you cast an illusion spell of 1st level or higher, you may choose one inanimate, nonmagical object that is part of the illusion and make that object real. You may do this on your turn as a bonus action while the spell is ongoing.

subclasses.name: School of Necromancy
subclasses.description: Study the forces of life and death

features.name: Grim Harvest
features.description: You receive the ability to reap life energy from creatures you kill with your spells. Once per turn when you kill one or more creatures with a spell of 1st level or higher, you regain hit points equal to twice the spell's level, or three times its level if the spell belongs to the School of Necromancy. You don't gain this benefit for killing constructs or undead.

features.name: Necromancy Savant
features.description: Copying necromancy spells into your spellbook costs half the usual gold and time.

features.name: Undead Thralls
features.description: You add the animate dead spell to your spellbook if it is not there already. When you cast animate dead, you may target one additional corpse or pile of bones, creating another zombie or skeleton, as appropriate. Whenever you create an undead using a necromancy spell, it has additional benefits: The creature's hit point maximum is increased by an amount equal to your wizard level.

features.name: Inured to Undeath
features.description: You have resistance to necrotic damage, and your hit point maximum can't be reduced.

features.name: Command Undead
features.description: You may use magic to bring undead under your control, even those created by other wizards. As an action, you may choose one undead that you may see within 60 feet of you. That creature makes a Charisma saving throw against your wizard spell save DC.

subclasses.name: School of Transmutation
subclasses.description: Study the ways magic can shift reality

features.name: Minor Alchemy
features.description: For each 10 minutes you spend performing the procedure, you may transform up to 1 cubic foot of material. After 1 hour, or until you lose your concentration (as if you were concentrating on a spell), the material reverts to its original substance.

features.name: Transmutation Savant
features.description: Copying transmutation spells into your spellbook costs half the usual gold and time.

features.name: Transmuter's Stone
features.description: When you create the stone, choose the benefit from the following options: Darkvision out to a range of 60 feet, as described in chapter 8. An increase to speed of 10 feet while the creature is unencumbered. Proficiency in Constitution saving throws.

features.name: Shapechanger
features.description: You add the polymorph spell to your spellbook, if it is not there already. You may cast polymorph without expending a spell slot. When you do so, you may target only yourself and transform into a beast whose challenge rating is 1 or lower.

features.name: Master Transmuter
features.description: You may use your action to consume the reserve of transmutation magic stored within your transmuter's stone in a single burst. Your transmuter's stone is destroyed and can't be remade until you finish a long rest. Panacea You remove all curses, diseases, and poisons affecting a creature that you touch with the transmuter's stone.

subclasses.name: School of Graviturgy
subclasses.description: Study the ways magic can shift and manipulate gravity.

features.name: Adjust Density
features.description: As an action, double or halve the weight of one Large or smaller object or creature within 30 feet for up to 1 minute while you concentrate. Halved weight improves speed and jumping but hinders Strength; doubled weight reduces speed but improves Strength. At 10th level, the target can be Huge or smaller.

features.name: Gravity Well
features.description: When a spell affects a creature, you can move that creature 5 feet to an unoccupied space if it is willing, the spell hits it with an attack, or it fails a saving throw against the spell.

features.name: Violent Attraction
features.description: Use your reaction to add 1d10 damage to a weapon attack made by a creature within 60 feet, or add 2d10 to falling damage within 60 feet. You can use this a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier and regain all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Event Horizon
features.description: As an action, create a gravitational field for up to 1 minute while concentrating. Hostile creatures that start their turns within 30 feet make Strength saves; on a failure they take 2d10 force damage and have speed 0, and on a success they take half damage and spend extra movement.

subclasses.name: School of Chronurgy
subclasses.description: Study the ways magic can shift and bend time itself.

features.name: Chronal Shift
features.description: Use your reaction after a creature within 30 feet makes an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw to force a reroll. The target must use the second result. You can use this twice and regain all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Temporal Awareness
features.description: Add your Intelligence modifier to initiative rolls.

features.name: Momentary Stasis
features.description: As an action, force a Large or smaller creature within 60 feet to make a Constitution save. On a failure it is incapacitated and has speed 0 until the end of your next turn or until it takes damage. Uses equal your Intelligence modifier, minimum one, return after a long rest.

features.name: Arcane Abeyance
features.description: Condense a spell cast with a slot of 4th level or lower into a gray bead for 1 hour. A creature holding the bead can use its action to release the spell using your spell attack bonus and save DC. You can create one bead per short or long rest.

features.name: Convergent Future
features.description: When you or a creature within 60 feet makes an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, use your reaction to replace the die result with the minimum needed to succeed or one less. You gain one level of exhaustion when you use this feature, removed only by a long rest.

subclasses.name: War Magic
subclasses.description: Study the ways magic can be used in combat and how it can be stored

features.name: Arcane Deflection
features.description: You have learned to weave your magic to fortify yourself against harm. When you are hit by an attack or you fail a saving throw, you may use your reaction to gain a +2 bonus to your AC against that attack or a +4 bonus to that saving throw. When you use this feature, you can't cast spells other than cantrips until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Tactical Wit
features.description: Your keen ability to assess tactical situations allows you to act quickly in battle. You may give yourself a bonus to your initiative rolls equal to your Intelligence modifier.

features.name: Power Surge
features.description: You may store magical energy within yourself to later empower your damaging spells. You may store a maximum number of power surges equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of one). Whenever you finish a long rest, your number of power surges resets to one.

features.name: Durable Magic
features.description: While you maintain concentration on a spell, you have a +2 bonus to AC and all saving throws.

features.name: Deflecting Shroud
features.description: Your Arcane Deflection becomes infused with deadly magic. When you use your Arcane Deflection feature, you may cause magical energy to arc from you. Up to three creatures of your choice that you may see within 60 feet of you each take force damage equal to half your wizard level.

subclasses.name: Bladesinging
subclasses.description: Study a tradition of wizardry that incorporates swordplay and dance.

features.name: Bladesinger Styles
features.description: Provides appearance or style guidance for the subclass; it has no standalone rules effect.

features.name: Bladesong
features.description: It graces you with supernatural speed, agility, and focus. You may use a bonus action to start the Bladesong, which lasts for 1 minute. It ends early if you are incapacitated, if you don medium or heavy armor or a shield, or if you use two hands to make an attack with a weapon.

features.name: Training in War and Song (Bladesinging)
features.description: When you adopt this tradition at 2nd level, you receive proficiency with light armor, and you receive proficiency with one type of one-handed melee weapon of your choice. You also gain proficiency in the Performance skill if you don't already have it.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Song of Defense
features.description: You may direct your magic to absorb damage while your bladesong is active. When you take damage, you may use your reaction to expend one spell slot and reduce that damage to you by an amount equal to five times the spell's slot level.

features.name: Song of Victory
features.description: You add your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1) to the damage of your melee weapon attacks while your Bladesong is active.

subclasses.name: Order of Scribes
subclasses.description: Study the ways magic can be enhanced through books of power

features.name: Awakened Spellbook
features.description: Using specially prepared inks and ancient incantations passed down by your wizardly order, you have awakened an arcane sentience within your spellbook. While you are holding the book, it grants you the following benefits: You may use the book as a spellcasting focus for your wizard spells.

features.name: Wizardly Quill
features.description: As a bonus action, you may magically create a Tiny quill in your free hand. When you write with it, it produces ink in a color of your choice on the writing surface. The time you must spend to copy a spell into your spellbook equals 2 minutes per spell level if you use the quill for the transcription.

features.name: Manifest Mind
features.description: You may conjure forth the mind of your Awakened Spellbook. As a bonus action while the book is on your person, you may cause the mind to manifest as a Tiny spectral object, hovering in an unoccupied space of your choice within 60 feet of you. The spectral mind is intangible and doesn't occupy its space, and it sheds dim light in a 10-foot radius.

features.name: Master Scrivener
features.description: Whenever you finish a long rest, you may create one magic scroll by touching your Wizardly Quill to a blank piece of paper or parchment and causing one spell from your Awakened Spellbook to be copied onto the scroll. The spellbook must be within 5 feet of you when you make the scroll. The chosen spell must be of 1st or 2nd level and must have a casting time of 1 action.

features.name: One with the Word
features.description: Your connection to your Awakened Spellbook has become so profound that your soul has become entwined with it. While the book is on your person, you have advantage on all Intelligence (Arcana) checks, as the spellbook helps you remember magical lore.
